   312	      )
   313	    self.custom_field_values =
   314	      issue.custom_field_values.to_h do |v|
   315	        [v.custom_field_id, v.value]
   316	      end
   317	    if options[:keep_status]
   318	      self.status = issue.status
   319	    end
   320	    self.author = User.current
   321	    unless options[:attachments] == false
   322	      self.attachments = issue.attachments.map do |attachement|
   323	        attachement.copy(:container => self)
   324	      end
   325	    end
   326	
   327	    unless options[:watchers] == false
   328	      self.watcher_user_ids = issue.visible_watcher_users.select{|u| u.status == User::STATUS_ACTIVE}.map(&:id)
   329	    end

   331	    @copy_options = options
   332	    self
   333	  end

   337	    copy = self.class.new.copy_from(self, copy_options)
   338	    copy.attributes = attributes if attributes

   383	
   384	  # Sets the tracker.
   385	  # This will set the status to the default status of the new tracker if:
   386	  # * the status was the default for the previous tracker
   387	  # * or if the status was not part of the new tracker statuses
   388	  # * or the status was nil
   389	  def tracker=(tracker)
   390	    tracker_was = self.tracker
   391	    association(:tracker).writer(tracker)
   392	    if tracker != tracker_was
   393	      if status == tracker_was.try(:default_status)
   394	        self.status = nil
   395	      elsif status && tracker && !tracker.issue_status_ids.include?(status.id)
   396	        self.status = nil
   397	      end
   398	      reassign_custom_field_values
   399	      @workflow_rule_by_attribute = nil
   400	    end
   401	    self.status ||= default_status
   402	    self.tracker
   403	  end

   405	  def project_id=(project_id)
   406	    if project_id.to_s != self.project_id.to_s
   407	      self.project = (project_id.present? ? Project.find_by_id(project_id) : nil)
   408	    end

   411	
   412	  # Sets the project.
   413	  # Unless keep_tracker argument is set to true, this will change the tracker
   414	  # to the first tracker of the new project if the previous tracker is not part
   415	  # of the new project trackers.
   416	  # This will:
   417	  # * clear the fixed_version is it's no longer valid for the new project.
   418	  # * clear the parent issue if it's no longer valid for the new project.
   419	  # * set the category to the category with the same name in the new
   420	  #   project if it exists, or clear it if it doesn't.
   421	  # * for new issue, set the fixed_version to the project default version
   422	  #   if it's a valid fixed_version.
   423	  def project=(project, keep_tracker=false)
   424	    project_was = self.project
   425	    association(:project).writer(project)
   426	    if project != project_was
   427	      @safe_attribute_names = nil
   428	    end
   429	    if project_was && project && project_was != project
   430	      @assignable_versions = nil
   431	
   432	      unless keep_tracker || project.trackers.include?(tracker)
   433	        self.tracker = project.trackers.first
   434	      end
   435	      # Reassign to the category with same name if any
   436	      if category
   437	        self.category = project.issue_categories.find_by_name(category.name)
   438	      end
   439	      # Clear the assignee if not available in the new project for new issues (eg. copy)
   440	      # For existing issue, the previous assignee is still valid, so we keep it
   441	      if new_record? && assigned_to && !assignable_users.include?(assigned_to)
   442	        self.assigned_to_id = nil
   443	      end
   444	      # Keep the fixed_version if it's still valid in the new_project
   445	      if fixed_version && fixed_version.project != project && !project.shared_versions.include?(fixed_version)
   446	        self.fixed_version = nil
   447	      end
   448	      # Clear the parent task if it's no longer valid
   449	      unless valid_parent_project?
   450	        self.parent_issue_id = nil
   451	      end
   452	      reassign_custom_field_values
   453	      @workflow_rule_by_attribute = nil
   454	    end
   455	    # Set fixed_version to the project default version if it's valid
   456	    if new_record? && fixed_version.nil? && project && project.default_version_id?
   457	      if project.shared_versions.open.exists?(project.default_version_id)
   458	        self.fixed_version_id = project.default_version_id
   459	      end
   460	    end
   461	    self.project
   462	  end

   541	  def safe_attribute_names(user=nil)
   542	    names = super
   543	    names -= disabled_core_fields
   544	    names -= read_only_attribute_names(user)
   545	    if new_record?
   546	      # Make sure that project_id can always be set for new issues
   547	      names |= %w(project_id)
   548	    end
   549	    if dates_derived?
   550	      names -= %w(start_date due_date)
   551	    end
   552	    if priority_derived?
   553	      names -= %w(priority_id)
   554	    end
   555	    if done_ratio_derived?
   556	      names -= %w(done_ratio)
   557	    end
   558	    names
   559	  end

   580	        p_id = p.to_i

   589	      end
   590	    end
   591	
   592	    if (t = attrs.delete('tracker_id')) && safe_attribute?('tracker_id')

   594	        self.tracker_id = t
   595	      end
   596	    end

   607	      self.tracker ||= allowed_trackers.first

   136	  # Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all issues visible by the specified user
   137	  def self.visible_condition(user, options={})
   138	    Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_issues, options) do |role, user|
   139	      sql =
   140	        if user.id && user.logged?
   141	          case role.issues_visibility
   142	          when 'all'
   143	            '1=1'
   144	          when 'default'
   145	            user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
   146	            "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false} " \
   147	              "OR #{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} " \
   148	              "OR #{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
   149	          when 'own'
   150	            user_ids = [user.id] + user.groups.pluck(:id).compact
   151	            "(#{table_name}.author_id = #{user.id} OR " \
   152	              "#{table_name}.assigned_to_id IN (#{user_ids.join(',')}))"
   153	          else
   154	            '1=0'
   155	          end
   156	        else
   157	          "(#{table_name}.is_private = #{connection.quoted_false})"
   158	        end
   159	      unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
   160	        tracker_ids = role.permissions_tracker_ids(:view_issues)
   161	        if tracker_ids.any?
   162	          sql = "(#{sql} AND #{table_name}.tracker_id IN (#{tracker_ids.join(',')}))"
   163	        else
   164	          sql = '1=0'
   165	        end
   166	      end
   167	      sql
   168	    end
   169	  end

   171	  # Returns true if usr or current user is allowed to view the issue
   172	  def visible?(usr=nil)
   173	    (usr || User.current).allowed_to?(:view_issues, self.project) do |role, user|
   174	      visible =
   175	        if user.logged?
   176	          case role.issues_visibility
   177	          when 'all'
   178	            true
   179	          when 'default'
   180	            !self.is_private? || (self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to))
   181	          when 'own'
   182	            self.author == user || user.is_or_belongs_to?(assigned_to)
   183	          else
   184	            false
   185	          end
   186	        else
   187	          !self.is_private?
   188	        end
   189	      unless role.permissions_all_trackers?(:view_issues)
   190	        visible &&= role.permissions_tracker_ids?(:view_issues, tracker_id)
   191	      end
   192	      visible
   193	    end
   194	  end
   195	
   196	  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to log time on the issue
   197	  def time_loggable?(user=User.current)
   198	    user.allowed_to?(:log_time, project) && (Setting.timelog_accept_closed_issues? || !closed?)
   199	  end

   201	  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to edit or add notes to the issue

   206	  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to edit the issue
   207	  def attributes_editable?(user=User.current)

   209	      user_tracker_permission?(user, :edit_own_issues) && author == user
   210	    )
   211	  end

The Issue model is the contract center: it owns the direct teardown rules for journals, time entries, relations, changesets, attachments, watchers, custom values, reactions, and webhooks through associations and acts_as modules. I’m now reading the callbacks and the controller entry points to separate inline effects from commit-time and async mail/webhook work.

  1338	  # Returns true if this issue blocks the other issue, otherwise returns false
  1339	  def blocks?(other)
  1340	    all = [self]
  1341	    last = [self]
  1342	    while last.any?
  1343	      current =
  1344	        last.map do |i|
  1345	          i.relations_from.where(:relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_BLOCKS).map(&:issue_to)
  1346	        end.flatten.uniq
  1347	      current -= last
  1348	      current -= all
  1349	      return true if current.include?(other)
  1350	
  1351	      last = current
  1352	      all += last
  1353	    end
  1354	    false
  1355	  end
  1356	
  1357	  # Returns true if the other issue might be rescheduled if the start/due dates of this issue change
  1358	  def would_reschedule?(other)
  1359	    all = [self]
  1360	    last = [self]
  1361	    while last.any?
  1362	      current = last.map do |i|
  1363	        i.relations_from.where(:relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_PRECEDES).map(&:issue_to) +
  1364	        i.leaves.to_a +
  1365	        i.ancestors.map {|a| a.relations_from.where(:relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_PRECEDES).map(&:issue_to)}
  1366	      end.flatten.uniq
  1367	      current -= last
  1368	      current -= all
  1369	      return true if current.include?(other)
  1370	
  1371	      last = current
  1372	      all += last
  1373	    end
  1374	    false
  1375	  end

  1402	  def soonest_start(reload=false)
  1403	    if @soonest_start.nil? || reload
  1404	      relations_to.reload if reload
  1405	      dates = relations_to.collect{|relation| relation.successor_soonest_start}
  1406	      p = @parent_issue || parent
  1407	      if p && Setting.parent_issue_dates == 'derived'
  1408	        dates << p.soonest_start
  1409	      end
  1410	      @soonest_start = dates.compact.max
  1411	    end
  1412	    @soonest_start
  1413	  end

  1415	  # Sets start_date on the given date or the next working day
  1416	  # and changes due_date to keep the same working duration.
  1417	  def reschedule_on(date)
  1418	    wd = working_duration
  1419	    date = next_working_date(date)
  1420	    self.start_date = date
  1421	    self.due_date = add_working_days(date, wd)
  1422	  end

  1424	  # Reschedules the issue on the given date or the next working day and saves the record.
  1425	  # If the issue is a parent task, this is done by rescheduling its subtasks.
  1426	  def reschedule_on!(date, journal=nil)
  1427	    return if date.nil?
  1428	
  1429	    if leaf? || !dates_derived?
  1430	      if start_date.nil? || start_date != date
  1431	        if start_date && start_date > date
  1432	          # Issue can not be moved earlier than its soonest start date
  1433	          date = [soonest_start(true), date].compact.max
  1434	        end
  1435	        if journal
  1436	          init_journal(journal.user)
  1437	        end
  1438	        reschedule_on(date)
  1439	        begin
  1440	          save
  1441	        rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError
  1442	          reload
  1443	          reschedule_on(date)
  1444	          save
  1445	        end
  1446	      end
  1447	    else
  1448	      leaves.each do |leaf|
  1449	        if leaf.start_date
  1450	          # Only move subtask if it starts at the same date as the parent
  1451	          # or if it starts before the given date
  1452	          if start_date == leaf.start_date || date > leaf.start_date
  1453	            leaf.reschedule_on!(date)
  1454	          end
  1455	        else
  1456	          leaf.reschedule_on!(date)
  1457	        end
  1458	      end
  1459	    end
  1460	  end

  1554	  def valid_parent_project?(issue=parent)
  1555	    return true if issue.nil? || issue.project_id == project_id
  1556	
  1557	    case Setting.cross_project_subtasks
  1558	    when 'system'
  1559	      true
  1560	    when 'tree'
  1561	      issue.project.root == project.root
  1562	    when 'hierarchy'
  1563	      issue.project.is_or_is_ancestor_of?(project) || issue.project.is_descendant_of?(project)
  1564	    when 'descendants'
  1565	      issue.project.is_or_is_ancestor_of?(project)
  1566	    else

  1571	  # Returns an issue scope based on project and scope
  1572	  def self.cross_project_scope(project, scope=nil)
  1573	    if project.nil?
  1574	      return Issue
  1575	    end
  1576	
  1577	    case scope
  1578	    when 'all', 'system'

  1580	    when 'tree'
  1581	      Issue.joins(:project).where("(#{Project.table_name}.lft >= :lft AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt <= :rgt)",
  1582	                                  :lft => project.root.lft, :rgt => project.root.rgt)
  1583	    when 'hierarchy'
  1584	      Issue.joins(:project).
  1585	        where(
  1586	          "(#{Project.table_name}.lft >= :lft AND " \
  1587	            "#{Project.table_name}.rgt <= :rgt) OR " \
  1588	            "(#{Project.table_name}.lft < :lft AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt > :rgt)",
  1589	          :lft => project.lft, :rgt => project.rgt
  1590	        )
  1591	    when 'descendants'
  1592	      Issue.joins(:project).where("(#{Project.table_name}.lft >= :lft AND #{Project.table_name}.rgt <= :rgt)",
  1593	                                  :lft => project.lft, :rgt => project.rgt)
  1594	    else
  1595	      Issue.where(:project_id => project.id)
  1596	    end
  1597	  end

  1628	  # Query generator for selecting groups of issue counts for a project
  1629	  # based on specific criteria
  1630	  #
  1631	  # Options
  1632	  # * project - Project to search in.
  1633	  # * with_subprojects - Includes subprojects issues if set to true.
  1634	  # * association - Symbol. Association for grouping.
  1635	  def self.count_and_group_by(options)
  1636	    assoc = reflect_on_association(options[:association])
  1637	    select_field = assoc.foreign_key
  1638	
  1639	    Issue.
  1640	      visible(User.current, :project => options[:project], :with_subprojects => options[:with_subprojects]).
  1641	      joins(:status).
  1642	      group(:status_id, :is_closed, select_field).
  1643	      count.
  1644	      map do |columns, total|
  1645	        status_id, is_closed, field_value = columns
  1646	        is_closed = ['t', 'true', '1'].include?(is_closed.to_s)
  1647	        {
  1648	          "status_id" => status_id.to_s,
  1649	          "closed" => is_closed,
  1650	          select_field => field_value.to_s,
  1651	          "total" => total.to_s
  1652	        }
  1653	      end
  1654	  end

  1658	    if parent_issue_id.present?
  1659	      scope = filter_projects_scope(Setting.cross_project_subtasks)
  1660	    end

  1662	    self.class.allowed_target_projects(user, project, scope)
  1663	  end

  1665	  # Returns a scope of projects that user can assign the issue to
  1666	  def allowed_target_projects(user=User.current, scope=nil)
  1667	    current_project = new_record? ? nil : project
  1668	    if scope
  1669	      scope = filter_projects_scope(scope)
  1670	    end
  1671	
  1672	    self.class.allowed_target_projects(user, current_project, scope)
  1673	  end
  1674	
  1675	  # Returns a scope of projects that user can assign issues to
  1676	  # If current_project is given, it will be included in the scope
  1677	  def self.allowed_target_projects(user=User.current, current_project=nil, scope=nil)
  1678	    condition = Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :add_issues)
  1679	    if current_project
  1680	      condition = ["(#{condition}) OR #{Project.table_name}.id = ?", current_project.id]
  1681	    end
  1682	
  1683	    if scope.nil?
  1684	      scope = Project
  1685	    end
  1686	
  1687	    scope.where(condition).having_trackers
  1688	  end

   198	  def update
   199	    return unless update_issue_from_params
   200	
   201	    attachments = params[:attachments] || params.dig(:issue, :uploads)
   202	    if @issue.attachments_addable?
   203	      @issue.save_attachments(attachments)
   204	    else
   205	      attachments = attachments.to_unsafe_hash if attachments.respond_to?(:to_unsafe_hash)
   206	      if [Hash, Array].any? { |klass| attachments.is_a?(klass) } && attachments.any?
   207	        flash[:warning] = l(:warning_attachments_not_saved, attachments.size)
   208	      end
   209	    end
   210	
   211	    saved = false
   212	    begin
   213	      saved = save_issue_with_child_records
   214	    rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError
   215	      @issue.detach_saved_attachments
   216	      @conflict = true
   217	      if params[:last_journal_id]
   218	        @conflict_journals = @issue.journals_after(params[:last_journal_id]).to_a
   219	        unless User.current.allowed_to?(:view_private_notes, @issue.project)
   220	          @conflict_journals.reject!(&:private_notes?)
   221	        end
   222	      end
   223	    end
   224	
   225	    if saved
   226	      render_attachment_warning_if_needed(@issue)
   227	      unless @issue.current_journal.new_record? || params[:no_flash]
   228	        flash[:notice] = l(:notice_successful_update)
   229	      end
   230	      respond_to do |format|
   231	        format.html do
   232	          redirect_back_or_default issue_path(@issue),
   233	            flash: { previous_and_next_issue_ids: previous_and_next_issue_ids_params }
   234	        end
   235	        format.api  {render_api_ok}
   236	      end
   237	    else
   238	      respond_to do |format|
   239	        format.html {render :action => 'edit'}
   240	        format.api  {render_validation_errors(@issue)}
   241	      end
   242	    end
   243	  end

   261	  # Bulk edit/copy a set of issues
   262	  def bulk_edit
   263	    @issues.sort!
   264	    @copy = params[:copy].present?
   265	    @notes = params[:notes]
   266	
   267	    if @copy
   268	      unless User.current.allowed_to?(:copy_issues, @projects)
   269	        raise ::Unauthorized
   270	      end
   271	    else
   272	      unless @issues.all?(&:attributes_editable?)
   273	        raise ::Unauthorized
   274	      end
   275	    end
   276	
   277	    edited_issues = Issue.where(:id => @issues.map(&:id)).to_a
   278	
   279	    @values_by_custom_field = {}
   280	    edited_issues.each do |issue|
   281	      issue.custom_field_values.each do |c|
   282	        if c.value_present?
   283	          @values_by_custom_field[c.custom_field] ||= []
   284	          @values_by_custom_field[c.custom_field] << issue.id
   285	        end
   286	      end
   287	    end
   288	
   289	    @allowed_projects = Issue.allowed_target_projects
   290	    if params[:issue]
   291	      @target_project = @allowed_projects.detect {|p| p.id.to_s == params[:issue][:project_id].to_s}
   292	      if @target_project
   293	        target_projects = [@target_project]
   294	        edited_issues.each {|issue| issue.project = @target_project}
   295	      end
   296	    end
   297	    target_projects ||= @projects
   298	
   299	    @trackers = target_projects.map {|p| Issue.allowed_target_trackers(p)}.reduce(:&)
   300	    if params[:issue]
   301	      @target_tracker = @trackers.detect {|t| t.id.to_s == params[:issue][:tracker_id].to_s}
   302	      if @target_tracker
   303	        edited_issues.each {|issue| issue.tracker = @target_tracker}
   304	      end
   305	    end
   306	
   307	    if @copy
   308	      # Copied issues will get their default statuses
   309	      @available_statuses = []
   310	    else
   311	      @available_statuses = edited_issues.map(&:new_statuses_allowed_to).reduce(:&)
   312	    end
   313	    if params[:issue]
   314	      @target_status = @available_statuses.detect {|t| t.id.to_s == params[:issue][:status_id].to_s}
   315	      if @target_status
   316	        edited_issues.each {|issue| issue.status = @target_status}
   317	      end
   318	    end
   319	
   320	    edited_issues.each do |issue|
   321	      issue.custom_field_values.each do |c|
   322	        if c.value_present? && @values_by_custom_field[c.custom_field]
   323	          @values_by_custom_field[c.custom_field].delete(issue.id)
   324	        end
   325	      end
   326	    end
   327	    @values_by_custom_field.delete_if {|k, v| v.blank?}
   328	    @custom_fields =
   329	      edited_issues.map{|i| i.editable_custom_fields}.
   330	        reduce(:&).select {|field| field.format.bulk_edit_supported}
   331	    @assignables = target_projects.map(&:assignable_users).reduce(:&)
   332	    @versions = target_projects.map {|p| p.shared_versions.open}.reduce(:&)
   333	    @categories = target_projects.map {|p| p.issue_categories}.reduce(:&)
   334	    if @copy
   335	      @attachments_present = @issues.detect {|i| i.attachments.any?}.present? &&
   336	                               (Setting.copy_attachments_on_issue_copy == 'ask')
   337	      @subtasks_present = @issues.detect {|i| !i.leaf?}.present?
   338	      @watchers_present = User.current.allowed_to?(:add_issue_watchers, @projects) &&
   339	                            Watcher.where(:watchable_type => 'Issue',
   340	                                          :watchable_id => @issues.map(&:id)).exists?
   341	    end
   342	
   343	    @safe_attributes = edited_issues.map(&:safe_attribute_names).reduce(:&)
   344	
   345	    @issue_params = params[:issue] || {}
   346	    @issue_params[:custom_field_values] ||= {}
   347	  end
   348	
   349	  def bulk_update
   350	    @issues.sort!
   351	    @copy = params[:copy].present?
   352	
   353	    attributes = parse_params_for_bulk_update(params[:issue])
   354	    copy_subtasks = (params[:copy_subtasks] == '1')
   355	    copy_watchers = (params[:copy_watchers] == '1')
   356	
   357	    if @copy
   358	      unless User.current.allowed_to?(:copy_issues, @projects)
   359	        raise ::Unauthorized
   360	      end

361	
   362	      target_projects = @projects
   363	      if attributes['project_id'].present?
   364	        target_projects = Project.where(:id => attributes['project_id']).to_a
   365	      end
   366	      unless User.current.allowed_to?(:add_issues, target_projects)
   367	        raise ::Unauthorized
   368	      end
   369	
   370	      unless User.current.allowed_to?(:add_issue_watchers, @projects)
   371	        copy_watchers = false
   372	      end
   373	    else
   374	      unless @issues.all?(&:attributes_editable?)
   375	        raise ::Unauthorized
   376	      end
   377	    end
   378	
   379	    unsaved_issues = []
   380	    saved_issues = []
   381	
   382	    if @copy && copy_subtasks
   383	      # Descendant issues will be copied with the parent task
   384	      # Don't copy them twice
   385	      @issues.reject! {|issue| @issues.detect {|other| issue.is_descendant_of?(other)}}
   386	    end
   387	
   388	    @issues.each do |orig_issue|
   389	      orig_issue.reload
   390	      if @copy
   391	        issue = orig_issue.copy(
   392	          {},
   393	          :attachments => copy_attachments?(params[:copy_attachments]),
   394	          :subtasks => copy_subtasks,
   395	          :watchers => copy_watchers,
   396	          :link => link_copy?(params[:link_copy])
   397	        )
   398	      else
   399	        issue = orig_issue
   400	      end
   401	      journal = issue.init_journal(User.current, params[:notes])
   402	      issue.safe_attributes = attributes
   403	      call_hook(:controller_issues_bulk_edit_before_save, {:params => params, :issue => issue})
   404	      if issue.save
   405	        saved_issues << issue
   406	      else
   407	        unsaved_issues << orig_issue
   408	      end
   409	    end
   410	
   411	    if unsaved_issues.empty?
   412	      flash[:notice] = l(:notice_successful_update) unless saved_issues.empty?
   413	      if params[:follow]
   414	        if @issues.size == 1 && saved_issues.size == 1
   415	          redirect_to issue_path(saved_issues.first)
   416	        elsif saved_issues.map(&:project).uniq.size == 1
   417	          redirect_to project_issues_path(saved_issues.map(&:project).first)
   418	        end
   419	      else
   420	        redirect_back_or_default _project_issues_path(@project)
   421	      end
   422	    else
   423	      @saved_issues = @issues
   424	      @unsaved_issues = unsaved_issues
   425	      @issues = Issue.visible.where(:id => @unsaved_issues.map(&:id)).to_a
   426	      bulk_edit
   427	      render :action => 'bulk_edit'
   428	    end
   429	  end
   430	
   431	  def destroy
   432	    raise Unauthorized unless @issues.all?(&:deletable?)
   433	
   434	    # all issues and their descendants are about to be deleted
   435	    issues_and_descendants_ids = Issue.self_and_descendants(@issues).pluck(:id)
   436	    time_entries = TimeEntry.where(:issue_id => issues_and_descendants_ids)
   437	    @hours = time_entries.sum(:hours).to_f
   438	
   439	    if @hours > 0
   440	      case params[:todo]
   441	      when 'destroy'
   442	        # nothing to do
   443	      when 'nullify'
   444	        if Setting.timelog_required_fields.include?('issue_id')
   445	          flash.now[:error] = l(:field_issue) + " " + ::I18n.t('activerecord.errors.messages.blank')
   446	          return
   447	        else
   448	          time_entries.update_all(:issue_id => nil)
   449	        end
   450	      when 'reassign'
   451	        reassign_to = @project && @project.issues.find_by_id(params[:reassign_to_id])
   452	        if reassign_to.nil?
   453	          flash.now[:error] = l(:error_issue_not_found_in_project)
   454	          return
   455	        elsif issues_and_descendants_ids.include?(reassign_to.id)
   456	          flash.now[:error] = l(:error_cannot_reassign_time_entries_to_an_issue_about_to_be_deleted)
   457	          return
   458	        else
   459	          time_entries.update_all(:issue_id => reassign_to.id, :project_id => reassign_to.project_id)
   460	        end
   461	      else
   462	        # display the destroy form if it's a user request
   463	        return unless api_request?
   464	      end
   465	    end
   466	    @issues.each do |issue|
   467	      begin
   468	        issue.reload.destroy
   469	      rescue ::ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound # raised by #reload if issue no longer exists
   470	        # nothing to do, issue was already deleted (eg. by a parent)
   471	      end
   472	    end
   473	    respond_to do |format|
   474	      format.html do
   475	        flash[:notice] = l(:notice_successful_delete)
   476	        redirect_back_or_default _project_issues_path(@project)
   477	      end
   478	      format.api  {render_api_ok}
   479	    end
   480	  end

   483	  # when the "New issue" tab is enabled
   484	  def current_menu_item
   485	    if Setting.new_item_menu_tab == '1' && [:new, :create].include?(action_name.to_sym)
   486	      :new_issue
   487	    else
   488	      super
   489	    end
   490	  end

   494	  def query_error(exception)
   495	    session.delete(:issue_query)
   496	    super
   497	  end
   498	
   499	  def retrieve_default_query(use_session)
   500	    return if params[:query_id].present?
   501	    return if api_request?
   502	    return if params[:set_filter]
   503	
   504	    if params[:without_default].present?
   505	      params[:set_filter] = 1
   506	      return
   507	    end
   508	    if !params[:set_filter] && use_session && session[:issue_query]
   509	      # Don't apply the default query if a valid query id is set in the session
   510	      query_id, project_id = session[:issue_query].values_at(:id, :project_id)
   511	      return if query_id && project_id == @project&.id && IssueQuery.exists?(id: query_id)
   512	    end
   513	    if default_query = IssueQuery.default(project: @project)
   514	      params[:query_id] = default_query.id
   515	    end
   516	  end
   517	
   518	  def retrieve_previous_and_next_issue_ids
   519	    if flash.key?(:previous_and_next_issue_ids)
   520	      flash[:previous_and_next_issue_ids].then do |info|
   521	        @prev_issue_id = info[:prev_issue_id].presence.try(:to_i)
   522	        @next_issue_id = info[:next_issue_id].presence.try(:to_i)
   523	        @issue_position = info[:issue_position].presence.try(:to_i)
   524	        @issue_count = info[:issue_count].presence.try(:to_i)
   525	      end
   526	      flash.delete(:previous_and_next_issue_ids)
   527	    else
   528	      retrieve_query_from_session
   529	      if @query
   530	        @per_page = per_page_option
   531	        limit = 500
   532	        issue_ids = @query.issue_ids(:limit => (limit + 1))
   533	        if (idx = issue_ids.index(@issue.id)) && idx < limit
   534	          if issue_ids.size < limit
   535	            @issue_position = idx + 1
   536	            @issue_count = issue_ids.size
   537	          end
   538	          @prev_issue_id = issue_ids[idx - 1] if idx > 0
   539	          @next_issue_id = issue_ids[idx + 1] if idx < (issue_ids.size - 1)
   540	        end
   541	        query_params = @query.as_params
   542	        if @issue_position
   543	          query_params = query_params.merge(:page => (@issue_position / per_page_option) + 1, :per_page => per_page_option)
   544	        end
   545	        @query_path = _project_issues_path(@query.project, query_params)
   546	      end
   547	    end
   548	  end
   549	
   550	  def previous_and_next_issue_ids_params
   551	    {
   552	      :prev_issue_id => params[:prev_issue_id],
   553	      :next_issue_id => params[:next_issue_id],
   554	      :issue_position => params[:issue_position],
   555	      :issue_count => params[:issue_count]
   556	    }.reject {|k, v| k.blank?}
   557	  end

   559	  # Used by #edit and #update to set some common instance variables
   560	  # from the params
   561	  def update_issue_from_params
   562	    @time_entry = TimeEntry.new(:issue => @issue, :project => @issue.project)
   563	    if params[:time_entry]
   564	      @time_entry.safe_attributes = params[:time_entry]
   565	    end
   566	    @issue.init_journal(User.current)
   567	    issue_attributes = params[:issue]
   568	    if issue_attributes && issue_attributes[:assigned_to_id] == 'me'
   569	      issue_attributes[:assigned_to_id] = User.current.id
   570	    end
   571	    if issue_attributes && params[:conflict_resolution]
   572	      case params[:conflict_resolution]
   573	      when 'overwrite'
   574	        issue_attributes = issue_attributes.dup
   575	        issue_attributes.delete(:lock_version)
   576	      when 'add_notes'
   577	        issue_attributes = issue_attributes.slice(:notes, :private_notes)
   578	      when 'cancel'
   579	        redirect_to issue_path(@issue)
   580	        return false
   581	      end
   582	    end
   583	    issue_attributes = replace_none_values_with_blank(issue_attributes)
   584	    @issue.safe_attributes = issue_attributes
   585	    @priorities = IssuePriority.active
   586	    @allowed_statuses = @issue.new_statuses_allowed_to(User.current)
   587	    true
   588	  end
   589	
   590	  # Used by #new and #create to build a new issue from the params
   591	  # The new issue will be copied from an existing one if copy_from parameter is given
   592	  def build_new_issue_from_params
   593	    @issue = Issue.new
   594	    if params[:copy_from]
   595	      begin
   596	        @issue.init_journal(User.current)
   597	        @copy_from = Issue.visible.find(params[:copy_from])
   598	        unless User.current.allowed_to?(:copy_issues, @copy_from.project)
   599	          raise ::Unauthorized
   600	        end
   601	
   602	        @link_copy = link_copy?(params[:link_copy]) || request.get?
   603	        @copy_attachments = copy_attachments?(params[:copy_attachments]) || request.get?
   604	        @copy_subtasks = params[:copy_subtasks].present? || request.get?
   605	        @copy_watchers = User.current.allowed_to?(:add_issue_watchers, @project)
   606	        @issue.copy_from(@copy_from, :attachments => @copy_attachments,
   607	                         :subtasks => @copy_subtasks, :watchers => @copy_watchers,
   608	                         :link => @link_copy)
   609	        @issue.parent_issue_id = @copy_from.parent_id
   610	      rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   611	        render_404
   612	        return
   613	      end
   614	    end
   615	    @issue.project = @project
   616	    if request.get?
   617	      @issue.project ||= @issue.allowed_target_projects.first
   618	    end
   619	    @issue.author ||= User.current
   620	    @issue.start_date ||= User.current.today if Setting.default_issue_start_date_to_creation_date?
   621	    offset = Setting.default_issue_due_date_offset_in_days
   622	    @issue.due_date ||= User.current.today + offset if offset
   623	
   624	    attrs = (params[:issue] || {}).deep_dup
   625	    if action_name == 'new' && params[:was_default_status] == attrs[:status_id]
   626	      attrs.delete(:status_id)
   627	    end
   628	    if action_name == 'new' && params[:form_update_triggered_by] == 'issue_project_id'
   629	      # Discard submitted version when changing the project on the issue form
   630	      # so we can use the default version for the new project
   631	      attrs.delete(:fixed_version_id)
   632	    end
   633	    if action_name == 'new' &&
   634	         %w[issue_project_id issue_tracker_id].include?(params[:form_update_triggered_by]) &&
   635	         attrs[:is_private] != '1'
   636	      # Drop the unchecked value so the selected tracker's private default can be applied.
   637	      attrs.delete(:is_private)
   638	    end
   639	    attrs[:assigned_to_id] = User.current.id if attrs[:assigned_to_id] == 'me'
   640	    @issue.safe_attributes = attrs
   641	
   642	    if @issue.project
   643	      @issue.tracker ||= @issue.allowed_target_trackers.first
   644	      if @issue.tracker.nil?
   645	        if @issue.project.trackers.any?
   646	          # None of the project trackers is allowed to the user
   647	          render_error :message => l(:error_no_tracker_allowed_for_new_issue_in_project), :status => 403
   648	        else
   649	          # Project has no trackers
   650	          render_error l(:error_no_tracker_in_project)
   651	        end
   652	        return false
   653	      end
   654	      if @issue.status.nil?
   655	        render_error l(:error_no_default_issue_status)
   656	        return false
   657	      end
   658	    elsif request.get?
   659	      render_error :message => l(:error_no_projects_with_tracker_allowed_for_new_issue), :status => 403
   660	      return false
   661	    end
   662	
   663	    @priorities = IssuePriority.active
   664	    @allowed_statuses = @issue.new_statuses_allowed_to(User.current)
   665	  end
   666	
   667	  # Saves @issue and a time_entry from the parameters
   668	  def save_issue_with_child_records
   669	    Issue.transaction do
   670	      if params[:time_entry] &&
   671	           (params[:time_entry][:hours].present? || params[:time_entry][:comments].present?) &&
   672	           User.current.allowed_to?(:log_time, @issue.project)
   673	        time_entry = @time_entry || TimeEntry.new
   674	        time_entry.project = @issue.project
   675	        time_entry.issue = @issue
   676	        time_entry.author = User.current
   677	        time_entry.user = User.current
   678	        time_entry.spent_on = User.current.today
   679	        time_entry.safe_attributes = params[:time_entry]
   680	        @issue.time_entries << time_entry
   681	      end
   682	      call_hook(
   683	        :controller_issues_edit_before_save,
   684	        {:params => params, :issue => @issue,
   685	         :time_entry => time_entry,
   686	         :journal => @issue.current_journal}
   687	      )
   688	      if @issue.save
   689	        call_hook(
   690	          :controller_issues_edit_after_save,
   691	          {:params => params, :issue => @issue,
   692	           :time_entry => time_entry,
   693	           :journal => @issue.current_journal}
   694	        )
   695	      else
   696	        raise ActiveRecord::Rollback
   697	      end
   698	    end
   699	  end
   700	
   701	  # Returns true if the issue copy should be linked
   702	  # to the original issue
   703	  def link_copy?(param)
   704	    case Setting.link_copied_issue
   705	    when 'yes'
   706	      true
   707	    when 'no'
   708	      false
   709	    when 'ask'
   710	      param == '1'
   711	    end
   712	  end
   713	
   714	  # Returns true if the attachments should be copied
   715	  # from the original issue
   716	  def copy_attachments?(param)
   717	    case Setting.copy_attachments_on_issue_copy
   718	    when 'yes'
   719	      true
   720	    when 'no'
   721	      false
   722	    when 'ask'
   723	      param == '1'
   724	    end
   725	  end
   726	
   727	  # Redirects user after a successful issue creation
   728	  def redirect_after_create
   729	    if params[:continue]
   730	      url_params = {}
   731	      url_params[:issue] = {
   732	        :tracker_id => @issue.tracker,
   733	        :parent_issue_id => @issue.parent_issue_id
   734	      }.compact
   735	      url_params[:back_url] = params[:back_url].presence
   736	
   737	      if params[:project_id]
   738	        redirect_to new_project_issue_path(@issue.project, url_params)
   739	      else
   740	        url_params[:issue][:project_id] = @issue.project_id
   741	        redirect_to new_issue_path(url_params)
   742	      end
   743	    elsif params[:follow]
   744	      redirect_to issue_path(@issue)
   745	    else
   746	      redirect_back_or_default issue_path(@issue)
   747	    end
   748	  end
   749	end

    46	  def create
    47	    saved = false
    48	    params_relation = params[:relation]
    49	    unsaved_relations = []
    50	
    51	    relation_issues_to_id.each do |issue_to_id|
    52	      params_relation[:issue_to_id] = issue_to_id
    53	
    54	      @relation = IssueRelation.new
    55	      @relation.issue_from = @issue
    56	      @relation.safe_attributes = params_relation
    57	      @relation.init_journals(User.current)
    58	
    59	      begin
    60	        saved = @relation.save
    61	      rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
    62	        @relation.errors.add :base, :taken
    63	      end
    64	
    65	      unsaved_relations << @relation unless saved
    66	    end
    67	
    68	    respond_to do |format|
    69	      format.html {redirect_to issue_path(@issue)}
    70	      format.js do
    71	        @relations = select_relations(@issue)
    72	        @unsaved_relations = unsaved_relations
    73	      end
    74	      format.api do
    75	        if saved
    76	          render :action => 'show', :status => :created, :location => relation_url(@relation)
    77	        else
    78	          render_validation_errors(@relation)
    79	        end
    80	      end
    81	    end

    85	    raise Unauthorized unless @relation.deletable?
    86	
    87	    @relation.init_journals(User.current)
    88	    @relation.destroy

    90	    respond_to do |format|
    91	      format.html {redirect_to issue_path(@relation.issue_from)}
    92	      format.js do

   100	  private
   101	
   102	  def find_issue
   103	    @issue = Issue.find(params[:issue_id])
   104	    @project = @issue.project
   105	  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   106	    render_404
   107	  end
   108	
   109	  def find_relation
   110	    @relation = IssueRelation.find(params[:id])
   111	  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   112	    render_404
   113	  end
   114	
   115	  def relation_issues_to_id
   116	    issue_to_id = params[:relation].require(:issue_to_id)
   117	    case issue_to_id
   118	    when String
   119	      issue_to_id = issue_to_id.split(',').reject(&:blank?)
   120	    when Integer
   121	      issue_to_id = [issue_to_id]
   122	    end
   123	    issue_to_id
   124	  rescue ActionController::ParameterMissing => e
   125	    # We return a empty array just to loop once and return a validation error
   126	    # ToDo: Find a better method to return an error if the param is missing.
   127	    ['']
   128	  end
   129	
   130	  def select_relations(issue)
   131	    issue.reload.relations.select {|r| r.other_issue(issue) && r.other_issue(issue).visible?}
   132	  end

    98	  def new
    99	    @time_entry ||=
   100	      TimeEntry.new(:project => @project, :issue => @issue,
   101	                    :author => User.current, :spent_on => User.current.today)
   102	    @time_entry.safe_attributes = params[:time_entry]

   104	
   105	  def create
   106	    @time_entry ||=
   107	      TimeEntry.new(:project => @project, :issue => @issue,
   108	                    :author => User.current, :user => User.current,
   109	                    :spent_on => User.current.today)
   110	    @time_entry.safe_attributes = params[:time_entry]
   111	    if @time_entry.project && !User.current.allowed_to?(:log_time, @time_entry.project)
   112	      render_403
   113	      return
   114	    end
   115	
   116	    call_hook(:controller_timelog_edit_before_save,
   117	              {:params => params, :time_entry => @time_entry})
   118	
   119	    if @time_entry.save
   120	      respond_to do |format|
   121	        format.html do
   122	          flash[:notice] = l(:notice_successful_create)
   123	          if params[:continue]
   124	            options = {
   125	              :time_entry => {
   126	                :project_id => params[:time_entry][:project_id],
   127	                :issue_id => @time_entry.issue_id,
   128	                :spent_on => @time_entry.spent_on,
   129	                :activity_id => @time_entry.activity_id
   130	              },
   131	              :back_url => params[:back_url]
   132	            }
   133	            if params[:project_id] && @time_entry.project
   134	              options[:time_entry][:project_id] ||= @time_entry.project.id
   135	              redirect_to new_project_time_entry_path(@time_entry.project, options)
   136	            elsif params[:issue_id] && @time_entry.issue
   137	              redirect_to new_issue_time_entry_path(@time_entry.issue, options)
   138	            else
   139	              redirect_to new_time_entry_path(options)
   140	            end
   141	          else
   142	            redirect_back_or_default project_time_entries_path(@time_entry.project)
   143	          end
   144	        end
   145	        format.api do
   146	          render :action => 'show', :status => :created, :location => time_entry_url(@time_entry)
   147	        end
   148	      end
   149	    else
   150	      respond_to do |format|
   151	        format.html {render :action => 'new'}
   152	        format.api  {render_validation_errors(@time_entry)}
   153	      end
   154	    end
   155	  end

   157	  def edit
   158	    @time_entry.safe_attributes = params[:time_entry]

   161	  def update
   162	    @time_entry.safe_attributes = params[:time_entry]
   163	    call_hook(:controller_timelog_edit_before_save,
   164	              {:params => params, :time_entry => @time_entry})
   165	
   166	    if @time_entry.save
   167	      respond_to do |format|
   168	        format.html do
   169	          flash[:notice] = l(:notice_successful_update)
   170	          redirect_back_or_default project_time_entries_path(@time_entry.project)
   171	        end
   172	        format.api  {render_api_ok}
   173	      end
   174	    else
   175	      respond_to do |format|

   177	        format.api  {render_validation_errors(@time_entry)}

   182	  def bulk_edit
   183	    @target_projects = Project.allowed_to(:log_time).to_a
   184	    @custom_fields = TimeEntry.first.available_custom_fields.select {|field| field.format.bulk_edit_supported}
   185	    if params[:time_entry]
   186	      @target_project = @target_projects.detect {|p| p.id.to_s == params[:time_entry][:project_id].to_s}
   187	    end
   188	    if @target_project
   189	      @available_activities = @target_project.activities
   190	    else
   191	      @available_activities = @projects.map(&:activities).reduce(:&)
   192	    end
   193	    @time_entry_params = params[:time_entry] || {}
   194	    @time_entry_params[:custom_field_values] ||= {}
   195	  end
   196	
   197	  def bulk_update
   198	    attributes = parse_params_for_bulk_update(params[:time_entry])
   199	
   200	    unsaved_time_entries = []
   201	    saved_time_entries = []
   202	
   203	    @time_entries.each do |time_entry|
   204	      time_entry.reload
   205	      time_entry.safe_attributes = attributes
   206	      call_hook(
   207	        :controller_time_entries_bulk_edit_before_save,
   208	        {:params => params, :time_entry => time_entry}
   209	      )
   210	      if time_entry.save
   211	        saved_time_entries << time_entry
   212	      else
   213	        unsaved_time_entries << time_entry
   214	      end
   215	    end
   216	
   217	    if unsaved_time_entries.empty?
   218	      flash[:notice] = l(:notice_successful_update) unless saved_time_entries.empty?
   219	      redirect_back_or_default project_time_entries_path(@projects.first)
   220	    else
   221	      @saved_time_entries = @time_entries
   222	      @unsaved_time_entries = unsaved_time_entries
   223	      @time_entries = TimeEntry.where(:id => unsaved_time_entries.map(&:id)).
   224	        preload(:project => :time_entry_activities).
   225	        preload(:user).to_a
   226	
   227	      bulk_edit
   228	      render :action => 'bulk_edit'
   229	    end
   230	  end
   231	
   232	  def destroy
   233	    destroyed = TimeEntry.transaction do
   234	      @time_entries.each do |t|
   235	        unless t.destroy && t.destroyed?
   236	          raise ActiveRecord::Rollback
   237	        end
   238	      end
   239	    end
   240	
   241	    respond_to do |format|
   242	      format.html do
   243	        if destroyed
   244	          flash[:notice] = l(:notice_successful_delete)
   245	        else
   246	          flash[:error] = l(:notice_unable_delete_time_entry)
   247	        end
   248	        redirect_back_or_default project_time_entries_path(@projects.first), :referer => true
   249	      end
   250	      format.api do
   251	        if destroyed
   252	          render_api_ok
   253	        else
   254	          render_validation_errors(@time_entries)
   255	        end
   256	      end
   257	    end
   258	  end
   259	
   260	  private

Create/update/destroy mostly run inline inside the web request; mail and webhook delivery are deferred from commit callbacks. I’m collecting the non-obvious dependents now: queries, reports, calendar/gantt, mail handlers, imports, repository commit parsing, helpers, APIs, and test coverage.

260	  private
   261	
   262	  def find_time_entry
   263	    @time_entry = TimeEntry.find(params[:id])
   264	    raise Unauthorized unless @time_entry.visible?
   265	
   266	    @project = @time_entry.project
   267	  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   268	    render_404
   269	  end
   270	
   271	  def check_editability
   272	    unless @time_entry.editable_by?(User.current)
   273	      render_403
   274	      return false
   275	    end
   276	  end
   277	
   278	  def find_time_entries
   279	    @time_entries = TimeEntry.where(:id => params[:id] || params[:ids]).
   280	      preload(:project => :time_entry_activities).
   281	      preload(:user).to_a
   282	
   283	    raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound if @time_entries.empty?
   284	    raise Unauthorized unless @time_entries.all? {|t| t.editable_by?(User.current)}
   285	
   286	    @projects = @time_entries.filter_map(&:project).uniq
   287	    @project = @projects.first if @projects.size == 1
   288	  rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound
   289	    render_404
   290	  end
   291	
   292	  def find_optional_issue
   293	    if params[:issue_id].present?
   294	      @issue = Issue.find(params[:issue_id])
   295	      @project = @issue.project
   296	      authorize
   297	    else
   298	      find_optional_project
   299	    end
   300	  end
   301	
   302	  # Returns the TimeEntry scope for index and report actions
   303	  def time_entry_scope(options={})
   304	    @query.results_scope(options)
   305	  end
   306	
   307	  def retrieve_time_entry_query
   308	    retrieve_query(TimeEntryQuery, false, :defaults => @default_columns_names)
   309	  end
   310	
   311	  def query_error(exception)
   312	    session.delete(:time_entry_query)
   313	    super
   314	  end
   315	end

    20	class TimeEntry < ApplicationRecord
    21	  include Redmine::SafeAttributes
    22	  # could have used polymorphic association
    23	  # project association here allows easy loading of time entries at project level with one database trip
    24	  belongs_to :project
    25	  belongs_to :issue
    26	  belongs_to :user
    27	  belongs_to :author, :class_name => 'User'
    28	  belongs_to :activity, :class_name => 'TimeEntryActivity'
    29	
    30	  acts_as_customizable
    31	  acts_as_event(
    32	    :title =>
    33	      Proc.new do |o|
    34	        related   = o.issue if o.issue && o.issue.visible?
    35	        related ||= o.project
    36	        "#{l_hours(o.hours)} (#{related.event_title})"
    37	      end,
    38	    :url =>
    39	      Proc.new do |o|
    40	        {:controller => 'timelog', :action => 'index', :project_id => o.project, :issue_id => o.issue}
    41	      end,
    42	    :author => :user,
    43	    :group => :issue,
    44	    :description => :comments
    45	  )

    49	  acts_as_webhookable

    52	  validates_presence_of :issue_id, :if => lambda {Setting.timelog_required_fields.include?('issue_id')}
    53	  validates_presence_of :comments, :if => lambda {Setting.timelog_required_fields.include?('comments')}
    54	  validates_numericality_of :hours, :allow_nil => true, :message => :invalid
    55	  validates_length_of :comments, :maximum => 1024, :allow_nil => true
    56	  validates :spent_on, :date => true
    57	  before_validation :set_project_if_nil
    58	  # TODO: remove this, author should be always explicitly set
    59	  before_validation :set_author_if_nil
    60	  validate :validate_time_entry
    61	
    62	  scope :visible, (lambda do |*args|
    63	    joins(:project).
    64	    where(TimeEntry.visible_condition(args.shift || User.current, *args))

    76	  end)

    79	                  'issue_id', 'activity_id', 'spent_on',

    86	  # Returns a SQL conditions string used to find all time entries visible by the specified user
    87	  def self.visible_condition(user, options={})
    88	    Project.allowed_to_condition(user, :view_time_entries, options) do |role, user|
    89	      if role.time_entries_visibility == 'all'
    90	        nil
    91	      elsif role.time_entries_visibility == 'own' && user.id && user.logged?
    92	        "#{table_name}.user_id = #{user.id}"
    93	      else
    94	        '1=0'
    95	      end
    96	    end
    97	  end
    98	
    99	  # Returns true if user or current user is allowed to view the time entry
   100	  def visible?(user=nil)
   101	    (user || User.current).allowed_to?(:view_time_entries, self.project) do |role, user|
   102	      if role.time_entries_visibility == 'all'
   103	        true
   104	      elsif role.time_entries_visibility == 'own'
   105	        self.user == user
   106	      else
   107	        false
   108	      end
   109	    end
   110	  end
   111	
   112	  def initialize(attributes=nil, *args)
   113	    super
   114	    if new_record? && self.activity.nil?
   115	      self.activity_id = TimeEntryActivity.default_activity_id(User.current, self.project)
   116	      self.hours = nil if hours == 0
   117	    end
   118	  end
   119	
   120	  def safe_attributes=(attrs, user=User.current)
   121	    if attrs
   122	      attrs = super(attrs)
   123	      if issue_id_changed? && issue
   124	        if issue.visible?(user) && user.allowed_to?(:log_time, issue.project)
   125	          if attrs[:project_id].blank? && issue.project_id != project_id
   126	            self.project_id = issue.project_id
   127	          end
   128	          @invalid_issue_id = nil
   129	        elsif user.allowed_to?(:log_time, issue.project) && issue.assigned_to_id_changed? && issue.previous_assignee == User.current
   130	          current_assignee = issue.assigned_to
   131	          issue.assigned_to = issue.previous_assignee
   132	          unless issue.visible?(user)
   133	            @invalid_issue_id = issue_id
   134	          end
   135	          issue.assigned_to = current_assignee
   136	        else
   137	          @invalid_issue_id = issue_id
   138	        end
   139	      end
   140	      if user_id_changed? && user_id != author_id && !user.allowed_to?(:log_time_for_other_users, project)
   141	        @invalid_user_id = user_id
   142	      else
   143	        @invalid_user_id = nil
   144	      end
   145	
   146	      # Delete assigned custom fields not visible by the user
   147	      editable_custom_field_ids = editable_custom_field_values(user).map {|v| v.custom_field_id.to_s}
   148	      self.custom_field_values.delete_if do |c|
   149	        !editable_custom_field_ids.include?(c.custom_field.id.to_s)
   150	      end
   151	    end
   152	
   153	    attrs
   154	  end
   155	
   156	  def set_project_if_nil
   157	    self.project = issue.project if issue && project.nil?
   158	  end
   159	
   160	  def set_author_if_nil
   161	    self.author = User.current if author.nil?
   162	  end
   163	
   164	  def validate_time_entry
   165	    if hours
   166	      errors.add :hours, :invalid if hours < 0
   167	      errors.add :hours, :invalid if hours == 0.0 && hours_changed? && !Setting.timelog_accept_0_hours?
   168	
   169	      max_hours = Setting.timelog_max_hours_per_day.to_f
   170	      if hours_changed? && max_hours > 0.0
   171	        logged_hours = other_hours_with_same_user_and_day
   172	        if logged_hours + hours > max_hours
   173	          errors.add(
   174	            :base,
   175	            I18n.t(:error_exceeds_maximum_hours_per_day,
   176	                   :logged_hours => format_hours(logged_hours),
   177	                   :max_hours => format_hours(max_hours)))
   178	        end
   179	      end
   180	    end
   181	    errors.add :project_id, :invalid if project.nil?
   182	    if @invalid_user_id || (user_id_changed? && user_id != author_id && !self.assignable_users.map(&:id).include?(user_id))
   183	      errors.add :user_id, :invalid
   184	    end
   185	    errors.add :issue_id, :invalid if (issue_id && !issue) || (issue && project!=issue.project) || @invalid_issue_id
   186	    errors.add :activity_id, :inclusion if activity_id_changed? && project && !project.activities.include?(activity)
   187	    if spent_on && spent_on_changed? && user
   188	      errors.add :base, I18n.t(:error_spent_on_future_date) if !Setting.timelog_accept_future_dates? && (spent_on > user.today)
   189	    end
   190	    if !Setting.timelog_accept_closed_issues? && issue&.closed? && issue.was_closed?
   191	      errors.add :base, I18n.t(:error_spent_on_closed_issue)
   192	    end
   193	  end
   194	
   195	  def hours=(h)
   196	    write_attribute :hours, (h.is_a?(String) ? (h.to_hours || h) : h)
   197	  end
   198	
   199	  def hours
   200	    h = read_attribute(:hours)
   201	    if h.is_a?(Float)
   202	      # Convert the float value to a rational with a denominator of 60 to
   203	      # avoid floating point errors.
   204	      #
   205	      # Examples:
   206	      #  0.38333333333333336 => (23/60)   # 23m
   207	      #  0.9913888888888889  => (59/60)   # 59m 29s is rounded to 59m
   208	      #  0.9919444444444444  => (1/1)     # 59m 30s is rounded to 60m
   209	      (h * 60).round / 60r
   210	    else
   211	      h
   212	    end
   213	  end
   214	
   215	  # tyear, tmonth, tweek assigned where setting spent_on attributes
   216	  # these attributes make time aggregations easier
   217	  def spent_on=(date)
   218	    super
   219	    self.tyear = spent_on ? spent_on.year : nil
   220	    self.tmonth = spent_on ? spent_on.month : nil
   221	    self.tweek = spent_on ? Date.civil(spent_on.year, spent_on.month, spent_on.day).cweek : nil
   222	  end
   223	
   224	  # Returns true if the time entry can be edited by usr, otherwise false
   225	  def editable_by?(usr)
   226	    visible?(usr) && (
   227	      (usr == user && usr.allowed_to?(:edit_own_time_entries, project)) || usr.allowed_to?(:edit_time_entries, project)
   228	    )
   229	  end
   230	
   231	  # Returns the custom_field_values that can be edited by the given user
   232	  def editable_custom_field_values(user=nil)
   233	    visible_custom_field_values(user)
   234	  end
   235	
   236	  # Returns the custom fields that can be edited by the given user
   237	  def editable_custom_fields(user=nil)
   238	    editable_custom_field_values(user).map(&:custom_field).uniq
   239	  end
   240	
   241	  def visible_custom_field_values(user = nil)
   242	    user ||= User.current
   243	    custom_field_values.select do |value|
   244	      value.custom_field.visible_by?(project, user)
   245	    end
   246	  end
   247	
   248	  def assignable_users
   249	    users = []
   250	    if project
   251	      user_ids =
   252	        project.members.active.preload(:roles).filter_map do |m|
   253	          m.roles.any? {|role| role.allowed_to?(:log_time)} ? m.user_id : nil
   254	        end.uniq
   255	      users = User.where(:id => user_ids).sorted.to_a
   256	    end
   257	    users << User.current if User.current.logged? && !users.include?(User.current)
   258	    users
   259	  end
   260	
   261	  private
   262	
   263	  # Returns the hours that were logged in other time entries for the same user and the same day
   264	  def other_hours_with_same_user_and_day
   265	    if user_id && spent_on
   266	      TimeEntry.
   267	        where(:user_id => user_id, :spent_on => spent_on).
   268	        where.not(:id => id).
   269	        sum(:hours).to_f
   270	    else
   271	      0.0
   272	    end
   273	  end
   274	end

    72	  validates_presence_of :issue_from, :issue_to, :relation_type
    73	  validates_inclusion_of :relation_type, :in => TYPES.keys
    74	  validates_numericality_of :delay, :allow_nil => true
    75	  validates_uniqueness_of :issue_to_id, :scope => :issue_from_id, :case_sensitive => true
    76	  validate :validate_issue_relation
    77	
    78	  before_save :handle_issue_order
    79	  after_create  :call_issues_relation_added_callback
    80	  after_destroy :call_issues_relation_removed_callback
    81	
    82	  safe_attributes 'relation_type',
    83	                  'delay',
    84	                  'issue_to_id'
    85	
    86	  def safe_attributes=(attrs, user=User.current)
    87	    if attrs.respond_to?(:to_unsafe_hash)
    88	      attrs = attrs.to_unsafe_hash
    89	    end
    90	    return unless attrs.is_a?(Hash)
    91	
    92	    attrs = attrs.deep_dup
    93	    if issue_id = attrs.delete('issue_to_id')
    94	      if issue_id.to_s.strip =~ /\A#?(\d+)\z/
    95	        issue_id = $1.to_i
    96	        self.issue_to = Issue.visible(user).find_by_id(issue_id)
    97	      end
    98	    end
    99	
   100	    super(attrs)
   101	  end
   102	
   103	  def visible?(user=User.current)
   104	    (issue_from.nil? || issue_from.visible?(user)) && (issue_to.nil? || issue_to.visible?(user))
   105	  end
   106	
   107	  def deletable?(user=User.current)
   108	    visible?(user) &&
   109	      ((issue_from.nil? || user.allowed_to?(:manage_issue_relations, issue_from.project)) ||
   110	        (issue_to.nil? || user.allowed_to?(:manage_issue_relations, issue_to.project)))
   111	  end
   112	
   113	  def initialize(attributes=nil, *args)
   114	    super
   115	    if new_record?
   116	      if relation_type.blank?
   117	        self.relation_type = IssueRelation::TYPE_RELATES
   118	      end
   119	    end
   120	  end
   121	
   122	  def validate_issue_relation
   123	    if issue_from && issue_to
   124	      errors.add :issue_to_id, :invalid if issue_from_id == issue_to_id
   125	      unless issue_from.project_id == issue_to.project_id ||
   126	                Setting.cross_project_issue_relations?
   127	        errors.add :issue_to_id, :not_same_project
   128	      end
   129	      if circular_dependency?
   130	        errors.add :base, :circular_dependency
   131	      end
   132	      if issue_from.is_descendant_of?(issue_to) || issue_from.is_ancestor_of?(issue_to)
   133	        errors.add :base, :cant_link_an_issue_with_a_descendant
   134	      end
   135	    end
   136	  end
   137	
   138	  def other_issue(issue)
   139	    (self.issue_from_id == issue.id) ? issue_to : issue_from
   140	  end
   141	
   142	  # Returns the relation type for +issue+
   143	  def relation_type_for(issue)
   144	    if TYPES[relation_type]
   145	      if self.issue_from_id == issue.id
   146	        relation_type
   147	      else
   148	        TYPES[relation_type][:sym]
   149	      end
   150	    end
   151	  end
   152	
   153	  def label_for(issue)
   154	    if TYPES[relation_type]
   155	      TYPES[relation_type][(self.issue_from_id == issue.id) ? :name : :sym_name]
   156	    else
   157	      :unknow
   158	    end
   159	  end
   160	
   161	  def to_s(issue=nil)
   162	    issue ||= issue_from
   163	    issue_text = block_given? ? yield(other_issue(issue)) : "##{other_issue(issue).try(:id)}"
   164	    s = []
   165	    s << l(label_for(issue))
   166	    s << "(#{l('datetime.distance_in_words.x_days', :count => delay)})" if delay && delay != 0
   167	    s << issue_text
   168	    s.join(' ')
   169	  end
   170	
   171	  def css_classes_for(issue)
   172	    "rel-#{relation_type_for(issue)}"
   173	  end
   174	
   175	  def handle_issue_order
   176	    reverse_if_needed
   177	
   178	    if relation_type == TYPE_PRECEDES
   179	      self.delay ||= 0
   180	    else
   181	      self.delay = nil
   182	    end
   183	    set_issue_to_dates
   184	  end
   185	
   186	  def set_issue_to_dates(journal=nil)
   187	    soonest_start = self.successor_soonest_start
   188	    if soonest_start && issue_to
   189	      issue_to.reschedule_on!(soonest_start, journal)
   190	    end
   191	  end
   192	
   193	  def successor_soonest_start
   194	    if (self.relation_type == TYPE_PRECEDES) && delay && issue_from &&
   195	           (issue_from.start_date || issue_from.due_date)
   196	      add_working_days((issue_from.due_date || issue_from.start_date), (1 + delay))
   197	    end
   198	  end
   199	
   200	  def <=>(relation)
   201	    return nil unless relation.is_a?(IssueRelation)
   202	
   203	    r = TYPES[self.relation_type][:order] <=> TYPES[relation.relation_type][:order]
   204	    r == 0 ? id <=> relation.id : r
   205	  end
   206	
   207	  def init_journals(user)
   208	    issue_from.init_journal(user) if issue_from
   209	    issue_to.init_journal(user) if issue_to
   210	  end
   211	
   212	  private
   213	
   214	  # Reverses the relation if needed so that it gets stored in the proper way
   215	  # Should not be reversed before validation so that it can be displayed back
   216	  # as entered on new relation form.
   217	  #
   218	  # Orders relates relations by ID, so that uniqueness index in DB is triggered
   219	  # on concurrent access.
   220	  def reverse_if_needed
   221	    if TYPES.has_key?(relation_type) && TYPES[relation_type][:reverse]
   222	      issue_tmp = issue_to
   223	      self.issue_to = issue_from
   224	      self.issue_from = issue_tmp
   225	      self.relation_type = TYPES[relation_type][:reverse]
   226	
   227	    elsif relation_type == TYPE_RELATES && issue_from_id > issue_to_id
   228	      self.issue_to, self.issue_from = issue_from, issue_to
   229	    end
   230	  end
   231	
   232	  # Returns true if the relation would create a circular dependency
   233	  def circular_dependency?
   234	    case relation_type
   235	    when 'follows'
   236	      issue_from.would_reschedule? issue_to
   237	    when 'precedes'
   238	      issue_to.would_reschedule? issue_from
   239	    when 'blocked'
   240	      issue_from.blocks? issue_to
   241	    when 'blocks'
   242	      issue_to.blocks? issue_from
   243	    when 'relates'
   244	      self.class.where(issue_from_id: issue_to, issue_to_id: issue_from).present?
   245	    else
   246	      false
   247	    end
   248	  end
   249	
   250	  def call_issues_relation_added_callback
   251	    call_issues_callback :relation_added
   252	  end
   253	
   254	  def call_issues_relation_removed_callback
   255	    call_issues_callback :relation_removed
   256	  end
   257	
   258	  def call_issues_callback(name)
   259	    [issue_from, issue_to].each do |issue|
   260	      if issue
   261	        issue.send name, self
   262	      end
   263	    end
   264	  end
   265	end

   212	  # Sets @custom_field instance variable on journals details using a single query
   213	  def self.preload_journals_details_custom_fields(journals)
   214	    field_ids = journals.map(&:details).flatten.select {|d| d.property == 'cf'}.map(&:prop_key).uniq
   215	    if field_ids.any?
   216	      fields_by_id = CustomField.where(:id => field_ids).index_by { |f| f.id }
   217	      journals.each do |journal|
   218	        journal.details.each do |detail|
   219	          if detail.property == 'cf'
   220	            detail.instance_variable_set :@custom_field, fields_by_id[detail.prop_key.to_i]
   221	          end
   222	        end
   223	      end
   224	    end
   225	    journals
   226	  end

   235	        [c.custom_field_id, c.value]
   236	      end
   237	    end
   238	    self
   239	  end

   241	  # Adds a journal detail for an attachment that was added or removed
   242	  def journalize_attachment(attachment, added_or_removed)
   243	    key = (added_or_removed == :removed ? :old_value : :value)
   244	    details <<
   245	      JournalDetail.new(
   246	        :property => 'attachment',
   247	        :prop_key => attachment.id,
   248	        key => attachment.filename
   249	      )
   250	  end
   251	
   252	  # Adds a journal detail for an issue relation that was added or removed
   253	  def journalize_relation(relation, added_or_removed)
   254	    key = (added_or_removed == :removed ? :old_value : :value)
   255	    details <<
   256	      JournalDetail.new(
   257	        :property  => 'relation',
   258	        :prop_key  => relation.relation_type_for(journalized),
   259	        key => relation.other_issue(journalized).try(:id)
   260	      )

   263	  private
   264	
   265	  # Generates journal details for attribute and custom field changes
   266	  def journalize_changes
   267	    # attributes changes
   268	    if @attributes_before_change
   269	      attrs = (journalized.journalized_attribute_names + @attributes_before_change.keys).uniq
   270	      attrs.each do |attribute|
   271	        before = @attributes_before_change[attribute]
   272	        after = journalized.send(attribute)
   273	        next if before == after || (before.blank? && after.blank?)
   274	
   275	        add_attribute_detail(attribute, before, after)
   276	      end
   277	    end
   278	    # custom fields changes
   279	    if @custom_values_before_change
   280	      values_by_custom_field_id = {}
   281	      @custom_values_before_change.each_key do |custom_field_id|
   282	        values_by_custom_field_id[custom_field_id] = nil
   283	      end
   284	      journalized.custom_field_values.each do |c|
   285	        values_by_custom_field_id[c.custom_field_id] = c.value
   286	      end
   287	
   288	      values_by_custom_field_id.each do |custom_field_id, after|
   289	        before = @custom_values_before_change[custom_field_id]
   290	        next if before == after || (before.blank? && after.blank?)
   291	
   292	        if before.is_a?(Array) || after.is_a?(Array)
   293	          before = [before] unless before.is_a?(Array)
   294	          after = [after] unless after.is_a?(Array)
   295	
   296	          # values removed
   297	          (before - after).reject(&:blank?).each do |value|
   298	            add_custom_field_detail(custom_field_id, value, nil)
   299	          end
   300	          # values added
   301	          (after - before).reject(&:blank?).each do |value|
   302	            add_custom_field_detail(custom_field_id, nil, value)
   303	          end
   304	        else
   305	          add_custom_field_detail(custom_field_id, before, after)
   306	        end
   307	      end
   308	    end
   309	    start
   310	  end
   311	
   312	  # Adds a journal detail for an attribute change
   313	  def add_attribute_detail(attribute, old_value, value)
   314	    add_detail('attr', attribute, old_value, value)
   315	  end
   316	
   317	  # Adds a journal detail for a custom field value change
   318	  def add_custom_field_detail(custom_field_id, old_value, value)
   319	    add_detail('cf', custom_field_id, old_value, value)
   320	  end
   321	
   322	  # Adds a journal detail
   323	  def add_detail(property, prop_key, old_value, value)
   324	    details <<
   325	      JournalDetail.new(
   326	        :property => property,
   327	        :prop_key => prop_key,
   328	        :old_value => old_value,
   329	        :value => value
   330	      )
   331	  end

   333	  def split_private_notes
   334	    if private_notes?
   335	      if notes.present?
   336	        if details.any?
   337	          # Split the journal (notes/changes) so we don't have half-private journals
   338	          journal = Journal.new(:journalized => journalized, :user => user, :notes => nil, :private_notes => false)
   339	          journal.details = details
   340	          journal.save
   341	          self.details = []
   342	          self.created_on = journal.created_on
   343	        end
   344	      else
   345	        # Blank notes should not be private
   346	        self.private_notes = false
   347	      end
   348	    end
   349	    true
   350	  end
   351	
   352	  def add_watcher
   353	    if user.is_a?(User) &&
   354	       user.pref.auto_watch_on?('issue_contributed_to') &&
   355	       valid_watcher?(user)
   356	      journalized.set_watcher(user, true)
   357	    end
   358	
   359	    assignee = journalized.assigned_to
   360	    if assignee.is_a?(User) &&
   361	       assignee.pref.auto_watch_on?('issue_assigned_to_me') &&
   362	       valid_watcher?(assignee)
   363	      journalized.set_watcher(assignee, true)
   364	    end
   365	  end
   366	
   367	  def valid_watcher?(user)
   368	    user.active? &&
   369	      user.allowed_to?(:add_issue_watchers, journalized.project) &&
   370	      journalized.valid_watcher?(user) &&
   371	      !journalized.watched_by?(user)
   372	  end
   373	
   374	  def send_notification
   375	    if notify? &&
   376	        (
   377	          Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_updated') ||
   378	          (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_note_added') && notes.present?) ||
   379	          (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_status_updated') && new_status.present?) ||
   380	          (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_assigned_to_updated') && detail_for_attribute('assigned_to_id').present?) ||
   381	          (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_priority_updated') && new_value_for('priority_id').present?) ||
   382	          (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_fixed_version_updated') && detail_for_attribute('fixed_version_id').present?) ||
   383	          (Setting.notified_events.include?('issue_attachment_added') && details.any? {|d| d.property == 'attachment' && d.value })
   384	        )
   385	      Mailer.deliver_issue_edit(self)
   386	    end
   387	  end
   388	
   389	  def select_journal_visible_user(notified)
   390	    if private_notes?
   391	      notified = notified.select {|user| user.allowed_to?(:view_private_notes, journalized.project)}
   392	    end
   393	    notified
   394	  end
   395	end

    39	  # Overrides ActionMailer::Base#process in order to set the recipient as the current user
    40	  # and his language as the default locale.
    41	  # The first argument of all actions of this Mailer must be a User (the recipient),
    42	  # otherwise an ArgumentError is raised.
    43	  def process(action, *args)
    44	    user = args.first
    45	    raise ArgumentError, "First argument has to be a user, was #{user.inspect}" unless user.is_a?(User)
    46	
    47	    initial_user = User.current
    48	    initial_language = ::I18n.locale
    49	    begin
    50	      User.current = user
    51	
    52	      lang = find_language(user.language) if user.logged?
    53	      lang ||= Setting.default_language
    54	      set_language_if_valid(lang)
    55	
    56	      super
    57	    ensure
    58	      User.current = initial_user
    59	      ::I18n.locale = initial_language
    60	    end
    61	  end

    65	  def self.default_url_options
    66	    options = {:protocol => Setting.protocol}
    67	    if Setting.host_name.to_s =~ /\A(https?\:\/\/)?(.+?)(\:(\d+))?(\/.+)?\z/i
    68	      host, port, prefix = $2, $4, $5
    69	      options.merge!(
    70	        {

    72	        }
    73	      )
    74	    else
    75	      options[:host] = Setting.host_name
    76	    end
    77	    options

    81	  def issue_add(user, issue)
    82	    redmine_headers 'Project' => issue.project.identifier,
    83	                    'Issue-Tracker' => issue.tracker.name,
    84	                    'Issue-Id' => issue.id,
    85	                    'Issue-Author' => issue.author.login,
    86	                    'Issue-Assignee' => assignee_for_header(issue)
    87	    redmine_headers 'Issue-Priority' => issue.priority.name if issue.priority
    88	    message_id issue
    89	    references issue
    90	    @author = issue.author
    91	    @issue = issue
    92	    @user = user
    93	    @issue_url = url_for(:controller => 'issues', :action => 'show', :id => issue)
    94	    subject = "[#{issue.project.name} - #{issue.tracker.name} ##{issue.id}]"
    95	    subject += " (#{issue.status.name})" if Setting.show_status_changes_in_mail_subject?
    96	    subject += " #{issue.subject}"
    97	    mail :to => user,
    98	      :subject => subject
    99	  end

   596	  # Sends reminders to issue assignees
   597	  # Available options:
   598	  # * :days     => how many days in the future to remind about (defaults to 7)
   599	  # * :tracker  => id of tracker for filtering issues (defaults to all trackers)
   600	  # * :project  => id or identifier of project to process (defaults to all projects)
   601	  # * :users    => array of user/group ids who should be reminded
   602	  # * :version  => name of target version for filtering issues (defaults to none)
   603	  def self.reminders(options={})
   604	    days = options[:days] || 7
   605	    project = options[:project] ? Project.find(options[:project]) : nil
   606	    tracker = options[:tracker] ? Tracker.find(options[:tracker]) : nil
   607	    target_version_id = options[:version] ? Version.named(options[:version]).pluck(:id) : nil
   608	    if options[:version] && target_version_id.blank?
   609	      raise ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound.new("Couldn't find Version named #{options[:version]}")
   610	    end
   611	
   612	    user_ids = options[:users]
   613	
   614	    scope =
   615	      Issue.open.where(
   616	        "#{Issue.table_name}.assigned_to_id IS NOT NULL" \
   617	          " AND #{Project.table_name}.status = #{Project::STATUS_ACTIVE}" \
   618	          " AND #{Issue.table_name}.due_date <= ?", days.day.from_now.to_date
   619	      )
   620	    scope = scope.where(:assigned_to_id => user_ids) if user_ids.present?
   621	    scope = scope.where(:project_id => project.id) if project
   622	    scope = scope.where(:fixed_version_id => target_version_id) if target_version_id.present?
   623	    scope = scope.where(:tracker_id => tracker.id) if tracker
   624	    issues_by_assignee = scope.includes(:status, :assigned_to, :project, :tracker).
   625	                              group_by(&:assigned_to)
   626	    issues_by_assignee.keys.each do |assignee|  # rubocop:disable Style/HashEachMethods
   627	      if assignee.is_a?(Group)
   628	        assignee.users.each do |user|
   629	          issues_by_assignee[user] ||= []
   630	          issues_by_assignee[user] += issues_by_assignee[assignee]
   631	        end
   632	      end
   633	    end
   634	
   635	    issues_by_assignee.each do |assignee, issues|
   636	      if assignee.is_a?(User) && assignee.active? && issues.present?
   637	        visible_issues = issues.select {|i| i.visible?(assignee)}
   638	        visible_issues.sort!{|a, b| (a.due_date <=> b.due_date).nonzero? || (a.id <=> b.id)}
   639	        reminder(assignee, visible_issues, days).deliver_later if visible_issues.present?
   640	      end

The dependency set is large but bounded around the tracker domain. I’m using targeted `rg` passes by class/route/table names and reading each hit’s owning file so the final map lists concrete dependents instead of vague categories.
