  1762	  # Callback for after the creation of an issue by copy
  1763	  # * adds a "copied to" relation with the copied issue
  1764	  # * copies subtasks from the copied issue
  1765	  def after_create_from_copy
  1766	    return unless copy? && !@after_create_from_copy_handled
  1767	
  1768	    if (@copied_from.project_id == project_id ||
  1769	          Setting.cross_project_issue_relations?) &&
  1770	        @copy_options[:link] != false
  1771	      if @current_journal
  1772	        @copied_from.init_journal(@current_journal.user)
  1773	      end
  1774	      relation =
  1775	        IssueRelation.new(:issue_from => @copied_from, :issue_to => self,
  1776	                          :relation_type => IssueRelation::TYPE_COPIED_TO)
  1777	      unless relation.save
  1778	        if logger
  1779	          logger.error(
  1780	            "Could not create relation while copying ##{@copied_from.id} to ##{id} " \
  1781	              "due to validation errors: #{relation.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
  1782	          )
  1783	        end
  1784	      end
  1785	    end
  1786	
  1787	    unless @copied_from.leaf? || @copy_options[:subtasks] == false
  1788	      copy_options = (@copy_options || {}).merge(:subtasks => false)
  1789	      copied_issue_ids = {@copied_from.id => self.id}
  1790	      @copied_from.reload.descendants.reorder("#{Issue.table_name}.lft").each do |child|
  1791	        # Do not copy self when copying an issue as a descendant of the copied issue
  1792	        next if child == self
  1793	        # Do not copy subtasks of issues that were not copied
  1794	        next unless copied_issue_ids[child.parent_id]
  1795	
  1796	        # Do not copy subtasks that are not visible to avoid potential disclosure of private data
  1797	        unless child.visible?
  1798	          if logger
  1799	            logger.error(
  1800	              "Subtask ##{child.id} was not copied during ##{@copied_from.id} copy " \
  1801	                "because it is not visible to the current user"
  1802	            )
  1803	          end
  1804	          next
  1805	        end
  1806	        copy = Issue.new.copy_from(child, copy_options)
  1807	        if @current_journal
  1808	          copy.init_journal(@current_journal.user)
  1809	        end
  1810	        copy.author = author
  1811	        copy.project = project
  1812	        copy.parent_issue_id = copied_issue_ids[child.parent_id]
  1813	        unless child.fixed_version.present? && child.fixed_version.status == 'open'
  1814	          copy.fixed_version_id = nil
  1815	        end
  1816	        unless child.assigned_to_id.present? &&
  1817	                 child.assigned_to.status == User::STATUS_ACTIVE
  1818	          copy.assigned_to = nil
  1819	        end
  1820	        unless copy.save
  1821	          if logger
  1822	            logger.error(
  1823	              "Could not copy subtask ##{child.id} " \
  1824	                "while copying ##{@copied_from.id} to ##{id} due to validation errors: " \
  1825	                "#{copy.errors.full_messages.join(', ')}"
  1826	            )
  1827	          end
  1828	          next
  1829	        end
  1830	        copied_issue_ids[child.id] = copy.id
  1831	      end
  1832	    end
  1833	    @after_create_from_copy_handled = true
  1834	  end

  1843	  # Updates the nested set for when an existing issue is moved
  1844	  def update_nested_set_attributes_on_parent_change
  1845	    former_parent_id = parent_id_before_last_save
  1846	    # delete invalid relations of all descendants
  1847	    self_and_descendants.each do |issue|
  1848	      issue.relations.each do |relation|
  1849	        relation.destroy unless relation.valid?
  1850	      end
  1851	    end
  1852	    # update former parent

  1863	  def recalculate_attributes_for(issue_id)
  1864	    if issue_id && p = Issue.find_by_id(issue_id)
  1865	      if p.priority_derived?
  1866	        # priority = highest priority of open children
  1867	        # priority is left unchanged if all children are closed and there's no default priority defined
  1868	        if priority_position =
  1869	             p.children.open.joins(:priority).maximum("#{IssuePriority.table_name}.position")
  1870	          p.priority = IssuePriority.find_by_position(priority_position)
  1871	        elsif default_priority = IssuePriority.default
  1872	          p.priority = default_priority
  1873	        end
  1874	      end
  1875	
  1876	      if p.dates_derived?
  1877	        # start/due dates = lowest/highest dates of children
  1878	        p.start_date = p.children.minimum(:start_date)
  1879	        p.due_date = p.children.maximum(:due_date)
  1880	        if p.start_date && p.due_date && p.due_date < p.start_date
  1881	          p.start_date, p.due_date = p.due_date, p.start_date
  1882	        end
  1883	      end
  1884	
  1885	      if p.done_ratio_derived?
  1886	        # done ratio = average ratio of children weighted with their total estimated hours
  1887	        unless Issue.use_status_for_done_ratio? && p.status && p.status.default_done_ratio
  1888	          children = p.children.to_a
  1889	          if children.any?
  1890	            child_with_total_estimated_hours = children.select {|c| c.total_estimated_hours.to_f > 0.0}
  1891	            if child_with_total_estimated_hours.any?
  1892	              average = Rational(
  1893	                child_with_total_estimated_hours.sum(&:total_estimated_hours).to_s,
  1894	                child_with_total_estimated_hours.count
  1895	              )
  1896	            else
  1897	              average = Rational(1)
  1898	            end
  1899	            done = children.sum do |c|
  1900	              estimated = Rational(c.total_estimated_hours.to_f.to_s)
  1901	              estimated = average unless estimated > 0.0
  1902	              ratio = c.closed? ? 100 : (c.done_ratio || 0)
  1903	              estimated * ratio
  1904	            end
  1905	            progress = Rational(done, average * children.count)
  1906	            p.done_ratio = progress.floor
  1907	          end
  1908	        end
  1909	      end
  1910	
  1911	      # ancestors will be recursively updated
  1912	      p.save(:validate => false)
  1913	    end
  1914	  end

  1916	  # Singleton class method is public
  1917	  class << self
  1918	    # Update issues so their versions are not pointing to a
  1919	    # fixed_version that is not shared with the issue's project
  1920	    def update_versions(conditions=nil)
  1921	      # Only need to update issues with a fixed_version from
  1922	      # a different project and that is not systemwide shared
  1923	      Issue.joins(:project, :fixed_version).
  1924	        where("#{Issue.table_name}.fixed_version_id IS NOT NULL" +
  1925	          " AND #{Issue.table_name}.project_id <> #{::Version.table_name}.project_id" +
  1926	          " AND #{::Version.table_name}.sharing <> 'system'").
  1927	        where(conditions).each do |issue|
  1928	        next if issue.project.nil? || issue.fixed_version.nil?
  1929	
  1930	        unless issue.project.shared_versions.include?(issue.fixed_version)
  1931	          retried = false
  1932	          begin
  1933	            issue.init_journal(User.current)
  1934	            issue.fixed_version = nil
  1935	            issue.save
  1936	          rescue ActiveRecord::StaleObjectError
  1937	            raise if retried
  1938	
  1939	            retried = true
  1940	            issue.reload
  1941	            retry
  1942	          end
  1943	        end
  1944	      end
  1945	    end
  1946	  end

  2010	    if Setting.close_duplicate_issues? && closing?

  2012	        # Reload is needed in case the duplicate was updated by a previous duplicate
  2013	        duplicate.reload

  2016	
  2017	        # Same user and notes
  2018	        if @current_journal
  2019	          duplicate.init_journal(@current_journal.user, @current_journal.notes)
  2020	          duplicate.private_notes = @current_journal.private_notes

  2055	  def create_parent_issue_journal
  2056	    return if persisted? && !saved_change_to_parent_id?
  2057	    return if destroyed? && @without_nested_set_update
  2058	
  2059	    child_id = self.id
  2060	    old_parent_id, new_parent_id =
  2061	      if persisted?
  2062	        [parent_id_before_last_save, parent_id]
  2063	      elsif destroyed?
  2064	        [parent_id, nil]
  2065	      else
  2066	        [nil, parent_id]
  2067	      end
  2068	
  2069	    if old_parent_id.present?
  2070	      Issue.transaction do
  2071	        if old_parent_issue = Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(old_parent_id)
  2072	          old_parent_issue.init_journal(User.current)
  2073	          old_parent_issue.current_journal.__send__(:add_attribute_detail, 'child_id', child_id, nil)
  2074	          old_parent_issue.save
  2075	        end
  2076	      end
  2077	    end
  2078	
  2079	    if new_parent_id.present?
  2080	      Issue.transaction do
  2081	        if new_parent_issue = Issue.visible.lock.find_by_id(new_parent_id)
  2082	          new_parent_issue.init_journal(User.current)
  2083	          new_parent_issue.current_journal.__send__(:add_attribute_detail, 'child_id', nil, child_id)
  2084	          new_parent_issue.save
  2085	        end
  2086	      end
  2087	    end
  2088	  end

    20	module Redmine
    21	  module NestedSet
    22	    module IssueNestedSet
    23	      def self.included(base)
    24	        base.class_eval do
    25	          belongs_to :parent, :class_name => self.name
    26	
    27	          before_create :add_to_nested_set, :if => lambda {|issue| issue.parent.present?}
    28	          after_create :add_as_root, :if => lambda {|issue| issue.parent.blank?}
    29	          before_update :handle_parent_change, :if => lambda {|issue| issue.parent_id_changed?}
    30	          before_destroy :destroy_children
    31	        end
    32	        base.extend ClassMethods
    33	        base.send :include, Redmine::NestedSet::Traversing
    34	      end
    35	
    36	      private
    37	
    38	      def target_lft
    39	        scope_for_max_rgt = self.class.where(:root_id => root_id).where(:parent_id => parent_id)
    40	        if id
    41	          scope_for_max_rgt = scope_for_max_rgt.where(id: ...id)
    42	        end
    43	        max_rgt = scope_for_max_rgt.maximum(:rgt)
    44	        if max_rgt
    45	          max_rgt + 1
    46	        elsif parent
    47	          parent.lft + 1
    48	        else
    49	          1
    50	        end
    51	      end
    52	
    53	      def add_to_nested_set(lock=true)
    54	        if lock
    55	          lock_nested_set { add_to_nested_set_without_lock }
    56	        else
    57	          add_to_nested_set_without_lock
    58	        end
    59	      end
    60	
    61	      def add_to_nested_set_without_lock
    62	        parent.send :reload_nested_set_values
    63	        self.root_id = parent.root_id
    64	        self.lft = target_lft
    65	        self.rgt = lft + 1
    66	        self.class.where(:root_id => root_id).where("lft >= ? OR rgt >= ?", lft, lft).update_all(
    67	          [
    68	            "lft = CASE WHEN lft >= :lft THEN lft + 2 ELSE lft END, " +
    69	              "rgt = CASE WHEN rgt >= :lft THEN rgt + 2 ELSE rgt END",
    70	            {:lft => lft}
    71	          ]
    72	        )
    73	      end
    74	
    75	      def add_as_root
    76	        self.root_id = id
    77	        self.lft = 1
    78	        self.rgt = 2
    79	        self.class.where(:id => id).update_all(:root_id => root_id, :lft => lft, :rgt => rgt)
    80	      end
    81	
    82	      def handle_parent_change
    83	        lock_nested_set do
    84	          reload_nested_set_values
    85	          if parent_id_was
    86	            remove_from_nested_set
    87	          end
    88	          if parent
    89	            move_to_nested_set
    90	          end
    91	          reload_nested_set_values
    92	        end
    93	      end
    94	
    95	      def move_to_nested_set
    96	        if parent
    97	          previous_root_id = root_id
    98	          self.root_id = parent.root_id
    99	
   100	          lft_after_move = target_lft
   101	          self.class.where(:root_id => parent.root_id).update_all(
   102	            [
   103	              "lft = CASE WHEN lft >= :lft THEN lft + :shift ELSE lft END, " +
   104	                "rgt = CASE WHEN rgt >= :lft THEN rgt + :shift ELSE rgt END",
   105	              {:lft => lft_after_move, :shift => (rgt - lft + 1)}
   106	            ]
   107	          )
   108	          self.class.where(:root_id => previous_root_id).update_all(
   109	            [
   110	              "root_id = :root_id, lft = lft + :shift, rgt = rgt + :shift",
   111	              {:root_id => parent.root_id, :shift => lft_after_move - lft}
   112	            ]
   113	          )
   114	          self.lft, self.rgt = lft_after_move, (rgt - lft + lft_after_move)
   115	          parent.send :reload_nested_set_values
   116	        end
   117	      end
   118	
   119	      def remove_from_nested_set
   120	        self.class.where(:root_id => root_id).where("lft >= ? AND rgt <= ?", lft, rgt).
   121	          update_all(["root_id = :id, lft = lft - :shift, rgt = rgt - :shift", {:id => id, :shift => lft - 1}])
   122	
   123	        self.class.where(:root_id => root_id).update_all(
   124	          [
   125	            "lft = CASE WHEN lft >= :lft THEN lft - :shift ELSE lft END, " +
   126	              "rgt = CASE WHEN rgt >= :lft THEN rgt - :shift ELSE rgt END",
   127	            {:lft => lft, :shift => rgt - lft + 1}
   128	          ]
   129	        )
   130	        self.root_id = id
   131	        self.lft, self.rgt = 1, (rgt - lft + 1)
   132	      end
   133	
   134	      def destroy_children
   135	        if @without_nested_set_update
   136	          children.each {|c| c.send :destroy_without_nested_set_update}
   137	          reload
   138	        else
   139	          lock_nested_set do
   140	            reload_nested_set_values
   141	            children.each {|c| c.send :destroy_without_nested_set_update}
   142	            reload
   143	            self.class.where(:root_id => root_id).where("lft > ? OR rgt > ?", lft, lft).update_all(
   144	              [
   145	                "lft = CASE WHEN lft > :lft THEN lft - :shift ELSE lft END, " +
   146	                  "rgt = CASE WHEN rgt > :lft THEN rgt - :shift ELSE rgt END",
   147	                {:lft => lft, :shift => rgt - lft + 1}
   148	              ]
   149	            )
   150	          end
   151	        end
   152	      end
   153	
   154	      def destroy_without_nested_set_update
   155	        @without_nested_set_update = true
   156	        destroy
   157	      end
   158	
   159	      def reload_nested_set_values
   160	        self.root_id, self.lft, self.rgt = self.class.where(:id => id).pick(:root_id, :lft, :rgt)
   161	      end
   162	
   163	      def save_nested_set_values
   164	        self.class.where(:id => id).update_all(:root_id => root_id, :lft => lft, :rgt => rgt)
   165	      end
   166	
   167	      def move_possible?(issue)
   168	        new_record? || !is_or_is_ancestor_of?(issue)
   169	      end
   170	
   171	      def lock_nested_set
   172	        if /sqlserver/i.match?(self.class.connection.adapter_name)
   173	          lock = "WITH (ROWLOCK HOLDLOCK UPDLOCK)"
   174	          # Custom lock for SQLServer
   175	          # This can be problematic if root_id or parent root_id changes
   176	          # before locking
   177	          sets_to_lock = [root_id, parent.try(:root_id)].compact.uniq
   178	          self.class.reorder(:id).where(:root_id => sets_to_lock).lock(lock).ids
   179	          yield
   180	        elsif Redmine::Database.mysql?
   181	          # Use a global lock to prevent concurrent modifications - MySQL row locks are broken, this will run into
   182	          # deadlock errors all the time otherwise.
   183	          # Trying to lock just the sets in question (by basing the lock name on root_id and parent&.root_id) will run
   184	          # into the same issues as the sqlserver branch above
   185	          Issue.with_advisory_lock!("lock_issues", timeout_seconds: 30) do
   186	            # still lock the issues in question, for good measure
   187	            sets_to_lock = [id, parent_id].compact
   188	            inner_join_statement = self.class.select(:root_id).where(id: sets_to_lock).distinct(:root_id).to_sql
   189	            self.class.reorder(:id).
   190	              joins("INNER JOIN (#{inner_join_statement}) as i2 ON #{self.class.table_name}.root_id = i2.root_id").
   191	              lock.ids
   192	
   193	            yield
   194	          end
   195	        else
   196	          sets_to_lock = [id, parent_id].compact
   197	          self.class.reorder(:id).where("root_id IN (SELECT root_id FROM #{self.class.table_name} WHERE id IN (?))", sets_to_lock).lock.ids
   198	          yield
   199	        end
   200	      end
   201	
   202	      def nested_set_scope
   203	        self.class.order(:lft).where(:root_id => root_id)
   204	      end
   205	
   206	      def same_nested_set_scope?(issue)
   207	        root_id == issue.root_id
   208	      end
   209	
   210	      module ClassMethods
   211	        def rebuild_tree!
   212	          transaction do
   213	            reorder(:id).lock.ids
   214	            update_all(:root_id => nil, :lft => nil, :rgt => nil)
   215	            where(:parent_id => nil).update_all(["root_id = id, lft = ?, rgt = ?", 1, 2])
   216	            roots_with_children = joins("JOIN #{table_name} parent ON parent.id = #{table_name}.parent_id AND parent.id = parent.root_id").distinct.pluck("parent.id")
   217	            roots_with_children.each do |root_id|
   218	              rebuild_nodes(root_id)
   219	            end
   220	          end
   221	        end
   222	
   223	        def rebuild_single_tree!(root_id)
   224	          root = Issue.where(:parent_id => nil).find(root_id)
   225	          transaction do
   226	            where(root_id: root_id).reorder(:id).lock.ids
   227	            where(root_id: root_id).update_all(:lft => nil, :rgt => nil)
   228	            where(root_id: root_id, parent_id: nil).update_all(["lft = ?, rgt = ?", 1, 2])
   229	            rebuild_nodes(root_id)
   230	          end
   231	        end
   232	
   233	        private
   234	
   235	        def rebuild_nodes(parent_id = nil)
   236	          nodes = where(:parent_id => parent_id, :rgt => nil, :lft => nil).order(:id).to_a
   237	
   238	          nodes.each do |node|
   239	            node.send :add_to_nested_set, false
   240	            node.send :save_nested_set_values
   241	            rebuild_nodes node.id
   242	          end
   243	        end
   244	      end
   245	    end
   246	  end
   247	end

    96	        def save_attachments(attachments, author=User.current)
    97	          if attachments.respond_to?(:to_unsafe_hash)
    98	            attachments = attachments.to_unsafe_hash
    99	          end
   100	
   101	          if attachments.is_a?(Hash)
   102	            attachments = attachments.stringify_keys
   103	            attachments = attachments.to_a.sort {|a, b|
   104	              if a.first.to_i > 0 && b.first.to_i > 0
   105	                a.first.to_i <=> b.first.to_i
   106	              elsif a.first.to_i > 0
   107	                1
   108	              elsif b.first.to_i > 0
   109	                -1
   110	              else
   111	                a.first <=> b.first
   112	              end
   113	            }
   114	            attachments = attachments.map(&:last)
   115	          end
   116	          if attachments.is_a?(Array)
   117	            @failed_attachment_count = 0
   118	            attachments.each do |attachment|
   119	              next unless attachment.present?
   120	              a = nil
   121	              if file = attachment['file']
   122	                a = Attachment.create(:file => file, :author => author)
   123	              elsif token = attachment['token'].presence
   124	                a = Attachment.find_by_token(token)
   125	                unless a
   126	                  @failed_attachment_count += 1
   127	                  next
   128	                end
   129	                a.filename = attachment['filename'] unless attachment['filename'].blank?
   130	                a.content_type = attachment['content_type'] unless attachment['content_type'].blank?
    25	      end

    27	      module ClassMethods
    28	        def acts_as_customizable(options = {})
    29	          return if self.included_modules.include?(Redmine::Acts::Customizable::InstanceMethods)

    57	        def custom_fields=(values)
    58	          values_to_hash = values.inject({}) do |hash, v|
    59	            v = v.stringify_keys
    60	            if v['id'] && v.has_key?('value')
    61	              hash[v['id']] = v['value']

    63	            hash

    68	        # Sets the values of the object's custom fields

    70	        def custom_field_values=(values)
    71	          values = values.stringify_keys
    72	
    73	          custom_field_values.each do |custom_field_value|
    74	            key = custom_field_value.custom_field_id.to_s
    75	            if values.has_key?(key)
    76	              custom_field_value.value = values[key]
    77	            end
    78	          end
    79	          @custom_field_values_changed = true
    80	        end
    81	
    82	        def custom_field_values
    83	          @custom_field_values ||= available_custom_fields.collect do |field|
    84	            x = CustomFieldValue.new
    85	            x.custom_field = field
    86	            x.customized = self
    87	            if field.multiple?
    88	              values = custom_values.select { |v| v.custom_field == field }
    89	              if values.empty?
    90	                values << custom_values.build(:customized => self, :custom_field => field)

    70	          # Searches the model for the given tokens and user visibility.
    71	          # The projects argument can be either nil (will search all projects), a project or an array of projects.
    72	          # Returns an array that contains the rank and id of all results.
    73	          # In current implementation, the rank is the record timestamp converted as an integer.
    74	          #
    75	          # Valid options:
    76	          # * :titles_only - searches tokens in the first searchable column only
    77	          # * :all_words - searches results that match all token
    78	          # * :
    79	          # * :limit - maximum number of results to return
    80	          #
    81	          # Example:
    82	          #   Issue.search_result_ranks_and_ids("foo")
    83	          #   # => [[1419595329, 69], [1419595622, 123]]
    84	          def search_result_ranks_and_ids(tokens, user=User.current, projects=nil, options={})
    85	            tokens = [] << tokens unless tokens.is_a?(Array)
    86	            projects = [] << projects if projects.is_a?(Project)
    87	
    88	            columns = searchable_options[:columns]
    89	            columns = columns[0..0] if options[:titles_only]

    92	            queries = 0
    93

    96	                search_scope(user, projects, options).
    97	                where(search_tokens_condition(columns, tokens, options[:all_words])),
    98	                options[:limit]
    99	              )
   100	              queries += 1
   101	              if !options[:titles_only] && searchable_options[:search_custom_fields]
   102	                searchable_custom_fields = CustomField.where(:type => "#{self.name}CustomField", :searchable => true).to_a
   103	                if searchable_custom_fields.any?
   104	                  fields_by_visibility = searchable_custom_fields.group_by {|field|
   105	                    field.visibility_by_project_condition(searchable_options[:project_key], user, "#{CustomValue.table_name}.custom_field_id")
   106	                  }
   107	                  clauses = []
   108	                  fields_by_visibility.each do |visibility, fields|
   109	                    clauses << "(#{CustomValue.table_name}.custom_field_id IN (#{fields.map(&:id).join(',')}) AND (#{visibility}))"
   110	                  end
   111	                  visibility = clauses.join(' OR ')

    20	class IssueImport < Import
    21	  AUTO_MAPPABLE_FIELDS = {
    22	    'tracker' => 'field_tracker',
    23	    'subject' => 'field_subject',
    24	    'description' => 'field_description',
    25	    'status' => 'field_status',
    26	    'priority' => 'field_priority',
    27	    'category' => 'field_category',
    28	    'assigned_to' => 'field_assigned_to',
    29	    'fixed_version' => 'field_fixed_version',
    30	    'is_private' => 'field_is_private',
    31	    'parent_issue_id' => 'field_parent_issue',
    32	    'start_date' => 'field_start_date',
    33	    'due_date' => 'field_due_date',
    34	    'estimated_hours' => 'field_estimated_hours',
    35	    'done_ratio' => 'field_done_ratio',
    36	    'unique_id' => 'field_unique_id',
    37	    'relation_duplicates' => 'label_duplicates',
    38	    'relation_duplicated' => 'label_duplicated_by',
    39	    'relation_blocks' => 'label_blocks',
    40	    'relation_blocked' => 'label_blocked_by',
    41	    'relation_relates' => 'label_relates_to',
    42	    'relation_precedes' => 'label_precedes',
    43	    'relation_follows' =>  'label_follows',
    44	    'relation_copied_to' => 'label_copied_to',
    45	    'relation_copied_from' => 'label_copied_from'
    46	  }
    47	
    48	  def self.menu_item
    49	    :issues
    50	  end
    51	
    52	  def self.authorized?(user)
    53	    user.allowed_to?(:import_issues, nil, :global => true) && user.allowed_to?(:add_issues, nil, :global => true)
    54	  end
    55	
    56	  # Returns the objects that were imported
    57	  def saved_objects
    58	    object_ids = saved_items.pluck(:obj_id)
    59	    objects = Issue.where(:id => object_ids).order(:id).preload(:tracker, :priority, :status)
    60	  end
    61	
    62	  # Returns a scope of projects that user is allowed to
    63	  # import issue to
    64	  def allowed_target_projects
    65	    Project.allowed_to(user, :import_issues)
    66	  end
    67	
    68	  def project
    69	    project_id = mapping['project_id'].to_i
    70	    allowed_target_projects.find_by_id(project_id) || allowed_target_projects.first
    71	  end
    72	
    73	  # Returns a scope of trackers that user is allowed to
    74	  # import issue to
    75	  def allowed_target_trackers
    76	    Issue.allowed_target_trackers(project, user)
    77	  end
    78	
    79	  def tracker
    80	    if mapping['tracker'].to_s =~ /\Avalue:(\d+)\z/
    81	      tracker_id = $1.to_i
    82	      allowed_target_trackers.find_by_id(tracker_id)
    83	    end
    84	  end
    85	
    86	  # Returns true if missing categories should be created during the import
    87	  def create_categories?
    88	    user.allowed_to?(:manage_categories, project) &&
    89	      mapping['create_categories'] == '1'
    90	  end
    91	
    92	  # Returns true if missing versions should be created during the import
    93	  def create_versions?
    94	    user.allowed_to?(:manage_versions, project) &&
    95	      mapping['create_versions'] == '1'
    96	  end
    97	
    98	  def mappable_custom_fields
    99	    if tracker
   100	      issue = Issue.new
   101	      issue.project = project
   102	      issue.tracker = tracker
   103	      issue.editable_custom_field_values(user).map(&:custom_field)
   104	    elsif project
   105	      project.all_issue_custom_fields
   106	    else
   107	      []
   108	    end
   109	  end
   110	
   111	  private
   112	
   113	  def build_object(row, item)
   114	    issue = Issue.new
   115	    issue.author = user
   116	    issue.notify = !!ActiveRecord::Type::Boolean.new.cast(settings['notifications'])
   117	
   118	    tracker_id = nil
   119	    if tracker
   120	      tracker_id = tracker.id
   121	    elsif tracker_name = row_value(row, 'tracker')
   122	      tracker_id = allowed_target_trackers.named(tracker_name).first.try(:id)
   123	    end
   124	
   125	    attributes = {
   126	      'project_id' => mapping['project_id'],
   127	      'tracker_id' => tracker_id,
   128	      'subject' => row_value(row, 'subject'),
   129	      'description' => row_value(row, 'description')
   130	    }
   131	    if status_name = row_value(row, 'status')
   132	      if status_id = IssueStatus.named(status_name).first.try(:id)
   133	        attributes['status_id'] = status_id
   134	      end
   135	    end
   136	    issue.send :safe_attributes=, attributes, user
   137	
   138	    attributes = {}
   139	    if priority_name = row_value(row, 'priority')
   140	      if priority_id = IssuePriority.active.named(priority_name).first.try(:id)
   141	        attributes['priority_id'] = priority_id
   142	      end
   143	    end
   144	    if issue.project && category_name = row_value(row, 'category')
   145	      if category = issue.project.issue_categories.named(category_name).first
   146	        attributes['category_id'] = category.id
   147	      elsif create_categories?
   148	        category = issue.project.issue_categories.build
   149	        category.name = category_name
   150	        if category.save
   151	          attributes['category_id'] = category.id
   152	        end
   153	      end
   154	    end
   155	    if assignee_name = row_value(row, 'assigned_to')
   156	      if assignee = Principal.detect_by_keyword(issue.assignable_users, assignee_name)
   157	        attributes['assigned_to_id'] = assignee.id
   158	      end
   159	    end
   160	    if issue.project && version_name = row_value(row, 'fixed_version')
   161	      version =
   162	        issue.project.versions.named(version_name).first ||
   163	        issue.project.shared_versions.named(version_name).first
   164	      if version
   165	        attributes['fixed_version_id'] = version.id
   166	      elsif create_versions?
   167	        version = issue.project.versions.build
   168	        version.name = version_name
   169	        if version.save
   170	          attributes['fixed_version_id'] = version.id
   171	        end
   172	      end
   173	    end
   174	    if is_private = row_value(row, 'is_private')
   175	      if yes?(is_private)
   176	        attributes['is_private'] = '1'
   177	      end
   178	    end
   179	    if parent_issue_id = row_value(row, 'parent_issue_id')
   180	      if parent_issue_id.start_with? '#'
   181	        # refers to existing issue
   182	        attributes['parent_issue_id'] = parent_issue_id[1..-1]
   183	      elsif use_unique_id?
   184	        # refers to other row with unique id
   185	        issue_id = items.where(:unique_id => parent_issue_id).first.try(:obj_id)
   186	
   187	        if issue_id
   188	          attributes['parent_issue_id'] = issue_id
   189	        else
   190	          add_callback(parent_issue_id, 'set_as_parent', item.position)
   191	        end
   192	      elsif /\A\d+\z/.match?(parent_issue_id)
   193	        # refers to other row by position
   194	        parent_issue_id = parent_issue_id.to_i
   195	
   196	        if parent_issue_id > item.position
   197	          add_callback(parent_issue_id, 'set_as_parent', item.position)
   198	        elsif issue_id = items.where(:position => parent_issue_id).first.try(:obj_id)
   199	          attributes['parent_issue_id'] = issue_id
   200	        end
   201	
   202	      else
   203	        # Something is odd. Assign parent_issue_id to trigger validation error
   204	        attributes['parent_issue_id'] = parent_issue_id
   205	      end
   206	    end
   207	    if start_date = row_date(row, 'start_date')
   208	      attributes['start_date'] = start_date
   209	    end
   210	    if due_date = row_date(row, 'due_date')
   211	      attributes['due_date'] = due_date
   212	    end
   213	    if estimated_hours = row_value(row, 'estimated_hours')
   214	      attributes['estimated_hours'] = estimated_hours
   215	    end
   216	    if done_ratio = row_value(row, 'done_ratio')
   217	      attributes['done_ratio'] = done_ratio
   218	    end
   219	
   220	    attributes['custom_field_values'] = issue.custom_field_values.inject({}) do |h, v|
   221	      value =
   222	        case v.custom_field.field_format
   223	        when 'date'
   224	          row_date(row, "cf_#{v.custom_field.id}")
   225	        else
   226	          row_value(row, "cf_#{v.custom_field.id}")
   227	        end
   228	      if value
   229	        h[v.custom_field.id.to_s] = v.custom_field.value_from_keyword(value, issue)
   230	      end
   231	      h
   232	    end
   233	
   234	    issue.send :safe_attributes=, attributes, user
   235	
   236	    if issue.tracker_id != tracker_id
   237	      issue.tracker_id = nil
   238	    end
   239	
   240	    issue
   241	  end
   242	
   243	  def extend_object(row, item, issue)
   244	    build_relations(row, item, issue)
   245	  end
   246	
   247	  def build_relations(row, item, issue)
   248	    IssueRelation::TYPES.each_key do |type|
   249	      has_delay = [IssueRelation::TYPE_PRECEDES, IssueRelation::TYPE_FOLLOWS].include?(type)
   250	
   251	      if decls = relation_values(row, "relation_#{type}")
   252	        decls.each do |decl|
   253	          unless decl[:matches]
   254	            # Invalid relation syntax - doesn't match regexp
   255	            next
   256	          end
   257	
   258	          if decl[:delay] && !has_delay
   259	            # Invalid relation syntax - delay for relation that doesn't support delays
   260	            next
   261	          end
   262	
   263	          relation = IssueRelation.new(
   264	            "relation_type" => type,
   265	            "issue_from_id" => issue.id
   266	          )
   267	
   268	          if decl[:other_id]
   269	            relation.issue_to_id = decl[:other_id]
   270	          elsif decl[:other_pos]
   271	            if use_unique_id?
   272	              issue_id = items.where(:unique_id => decl[:other_pos]).first.try(:obj_id)
   273	              if issue_id
   274	                relation.issue_to_id = issue_id
   275	              else
   276	                add_callback(decl[:other_pos], 'set_relation', item.position, type, decl[:delay])
   277	                next
   278	              end
   279	            elsif decl[:other_pos] > item.position
   280	              add_callback(decl[:other_pos], 'set_relation', item.position, type, decl[:delay])
   281	              next
   282	            elsif issue_id = items.where(:position => decl[:other_pos]).first.try(:obj_id)
   283	              relation.issue_to_id = issue_id
   284	            end
   285	          end
   286	
   287	          relation.delay = decl[:delay] if decl[:delay]
   288	
   289	          begin
   290	            relation.save!
   291	          rescue
   292	            nil
   293	          end
   294	        end
   295	      end
   296	    end
   297	
   298	    issue
   299	  end
   300	
   301	  def relation_values(row, name)
   302	    content = row_value(row, name)
   303	
   304	    return if content.blank?
   305	
   306	    content.split(",").map do |declaration|
   307	      declaration = declaration.strip
   308	
   309	      # Valid expression:
   310	      #
   311	      # 123  => row 123 within the CSV
   312	      # #123 => issue with ID 123
   313	      #
   314	      # For precedes and follows
   315	      #
   316	      # 123 7d    => row 123 within CSV with 7 day delay
   317	      # #123  7d  => issue with ID 123 with 7 day delay
   318	      # 123 -3d   => negative delay allowed
   319	      #
   320	      #
   321	      # Invalid expression:
   322	      #
   323	      # No. 123 => Invalid leading letters
   324	      # # 123   => Invalid space between # and issue number
   325	      # 123 8h  => No other time units allowed (just days)
   326	      #
   327	      # Please note: If unique_id mapping is present, the whole line - but the
   328	      # trailing delay expression - is considered unique_id.
   329	      #
   330	      # See examples at Rubular http://rubular.com/r/mgXM5Rp6zK
   331	      #
   332	      match = declaration.match(/\A(?<unique_id>(?<is_id>#)?(?<id>\d+)|.+?)(?:\s+(?<delay>-?\d+)d)?\z/)
   333	
   334	      result = {
   335	        :matches     => false,
   336	        :declaration => declaration
   337	      }
   338	
   339	      if match
   340	        result[:matches] = true
   341	        result[:delay]   = match[:delay]
   342	
   343	        if match[:is_id] && match[:id]
   344	          result[:other_id] = match[:id]
   345	        elsif use_unique_id? && match[:unique_id]
   346	          result[:other_pos] = match[:unique_id]
   347	        elsif match[:id]
   348	          result[:other_pos] = match[:id].to_i
   349	        else
   350	          result[:matches] = false
   351	        end
   352	      end
   353	
   354	      result
   355	    end
   356	  end
   357	
   358	  # Callback that sets issue as the parent of a previously imported issue
   359	  def set_as_parent_callback(issue, child_position)
   360	    child_id = items.where(:position => child_position).first.try(:obj_id)
   361	    return unless child_id
   362	
   363	    child = Issue.find_by_id(child_id)
   364	    return unless child
   365	
   366	    child.parent_issue_id = issue.id
   367	    child.save!
   368	    issue.reload
   369	  end
   370	
   371	  def set_relation_callback(to_issue, from_position, type, delay)
   372	    return if to_issue.new_record?
   373	
   374	    from_id = items.where(:position => from_position).first.try(:obj_id)
   375	    return unless from_id
   376	
   377	    IssueRelation.create!(
   378	      'relation_type' => type,
   379	      'issue_from_id' => from_id,
   380	      'issue_to_id'   => to_issue.id,
   381	      'delay'         => delay
   382	    )
   383	    to_issue.reload
   384	  end
   385	end

   101	  def before_create_cs
   102	    self.committer = self.class.to_utf8(self.committer, repository.repo_log_encoding)
   103	    self.comments =
   104	      self.class.normalize_comments(self.comments, repository.repo_log_encoding)

   106	  end
   107	
   108	  def scan_for_issues
   109	    scan_comment_for_issue_ids
   110	  end
   111	
   112	  TIMELOG_RE = /
   113	    (
   114	    ((\d+)(h|hours?))((\d+)(m|min)?)?
   115	    |
   116	    ((\d+)(h|hours?|m|min))
   117	    |
   118	    (\d+):(\d+)
   119	    |
   120	    (\d+([.,]\d+)?)h?
   121	    )
   122	    /x
   123	
   124	  def scan_comment_for_issue_ids
   125	    return if comments.blank?
   126	
   127	    # keywords used to reference issues
   128	    ref_keywords = Setting.commit_ref_keywords.downcase.split(",").collect(&:strip)
   129	    ref_keywords_any = ref_keywords.delete('*')
   130	
   131	    # keywords used to fix issues
   132	    fix_keywords = Setting.commit_update_keywords_array.pluck('keywords').flatten.compact
   133	    kw_regexp = (ref_keywords + fix_keywords).collect{|kw| Regexp.escape(kw)}.join("|")
   134	
   135	    referenced_issues = []
   136	    regexp =
   137	      %r{
   138	        ([\s(\[,-]|^)((#{kw_regexp})[\s:]+)?
   139	        (\#\d+(\s+@#{TIMELOG_RE})?([\s,;&]+\#\d+(\s+@#{TIMELOG_RE})?)*)
   140	        (?=[[:punct:]]|\s|<|$)
   141	      }xi
   142	    comments.scan(regexp) do |match|
   143	      action = match[2].to_s.downcase
   144	      refs   = match[3]
   145	      next unless action.present? || ref_keywords_any
   146	
   147	      refs.scan(/#(\d+)(\s+@#{TIMELOG_RE})?/o).each do |m|
   148	        issue = find_referenced_issue_by_id(m[0].to_i)
   149	        hours = m[2]
   150	        if issue && !issue_linked_to_same_commit?(issue)
   151	          referenced_issues << issue
   152	          # Don't update issues or log time when importing old commits
   153	          unless repository.created_on && committed_on && committed_on < repository.created_on
   154	            fix_issue(issue, action) if fix_keywords.include?(action)
   155	            log_time(issue, hours) if hours && Setting.commit_logtime_enabled?
   156	          end
   157	        end
   158	      end
   159	    end
   160	
   161	    referenced_issues.uniq!
   162	    self.issues = referenced_issues unless referenced_issues.empty?
   163	  end
   164	
   165	  def short_comments
   166	    @short_comments || split_comments.first
   167	  end
   168	
   169	  def long_comments
   170	    @long_comments || split_comments.last
   171	  end

   173	  def text_tag(ref_project=nil)
   174	    repo = ""
   175	    if repository && repository.identifier.present?
   176	      repo = "#{repository.identifier}|"
   177	    end
   178	    tag = scmid? ? "commit:#{repo}#{scmid}" : "#{repo}r#{revision}"
   179	    if ref_project && project && ref_project != project
   180	      tag = "#{project.identifier}:#{tag}"
   181	    end
   182	    tag
   183	  end

   185	  # Returns the title used for the changeset in the activity/search results
   186	  def title
   187	    repo = (repository && repository.identifier.present?) ? " (#{repository.identifier})" : ''
   188	    comm = short_comments.blank? ? '' : (': ' + short_comments)
   189	    "#{l(:label_revision)} #{format_identifier}#{repo}#{comm}"
   190	  end

   202	  # Creates a new Change from it's common parameters
   203	  def create_change(change)
   204	    Change.create(:changeset     => self,
   205	                  :action        => change[:action],
   206	                  :path          => change[:path],
   207	                  :from_path     => change[:from_path],
   208	                  :from_revision => change[:from_revision])
   209	  end
   210	
   211	  # Finds an issue that can be referenced by the commit message
   212	  def find_referenced_issue_by_id(id)
   213	    return nil if id.blank?
   214	
   215	    issue = Issue.find_by_id(id.to_i)
   216	    if Setting.commit_cross_project_ref?
   217	      # all issues can be referenced/fixed
   218	    elsif issue
   219	      # issue that belong to the repository project, a subproject or a parent project only
   220	      unless issue.project &&
   221	                (project == issue.project || project.is_ancestor_of?(issue.project) ||
   222	                 project.is_descendant_of?(issue.project))
   223	        issue = nil
   224	      end
   225	    end
   226	    issue
   227	  end
   228	
   229	  private
   230	
   231	  # Returns true if the issue is already linked to the same commit
   232	  # from a different repository
   233	  def issue_linked_to_same_commit?(issue)
   234	    repository.same_commits_in_scope(issue.changesets, self).any?
   235	  end
   236	
   237	  # Updates the +issue+ according to +action+
   238	  def fix_issue(issue, action)
   239	    # the issue may have been updated by the closure of another one (eg. duplicate)
   240	    issue.reload
   241	    # don't change the status is the issue is closed
   242	    return if issue.closed?
   243	
   244	    journal = issue.init_journal(user || User.anonymous,
   245	                                 ll(Setting.default_language,
   246	                                    :text_status_changed_by_changeset,
   247	                                    text_tag(issue.project)))
   248	    rule = Setting.commit_update_keywords_array.detect do |rule|
   249	      rule['keywords'].include?(action) &&
   250	        (rule['if_tracker_id'].blank? || rule['if_tracker_id'] == issue.tracker_id.to_s)
   251	    end
   252	    if rule
   253	      issue.assign_attributes rule.slice(*Issue.attribute_names)
   254	    end
   255	    Redmine::Hook.call_hook(:model_changeset_scan_commit_for_issue_ids_pre_issue_update,
   256	                            {:changeset => self, :issue => issue, :action => action})
   257	
   258	    if issue.changes.any?
   259	      unless issue.save
   260	        logger.warn("Issue ##{issue.id} could not be saved by changeset #{id}: #{issue.errors.full_messages}") if logger
   261	      end
   262	    else
   263	      issue.clear_journal
   264	    end
   265	    issue
   266	  end
   267	
   268	  def log_time(issue, hours)
   269	    time_entry =
   270	      TimeEntry.new(
   271	        :user => user,
   272	        :hours => hours,
   273	        :issue => issue,
   274	        :spent_on => commit_date,
   275	        :comments => l(:text_time_logged_by_changeset, :value => text_tag(issue.project),
   276	                       :locale => Setting.default_language)
   277	      )
   278	    if activity = issue.project.commit_logtime_activity
   279	      time_entry.activity = activity
   280	    end
   281	
   282	    unless time_entry.save
   283	      logger.warn("TimeEntry could not be created by changeset #{id}: #{time_entry.errors.full_messages}") if logger
   284	    end
   285	    time_entry
   286	  end
   287	
   288	  def split_comments
   289	    comments =~ /\A(.+?)\r?\n(.*)$/m
   290	    @short_comments = $1 || comments
   291	    @long_comments = $2.to_s.strip
   292	    [@short_comments, @long_comments]
   293	  end

   295	  # Singleton class method is public
   296	  class << self
   297	    # Strips and reencodes a commit log before insertion into the database
   298	    def normalize_comments(str, encoding)
   299	      Changeset.to_utf8(str.to_s, encoding).strip
   300	    end
   301	
   302	    def to_utf8(str, encoding)
   303	      Redmine::CodesetUtil.to_utf8(str, encoding)
   304	    end
   305	  end
   306	end

    85	  def self.default(project: nil, user: User.current)
    86	    # user default
    87	    if user&.logged? && (query_id = user.pref.default_issue_query).present?
    88	      query = find_by(id: query_id)
    89	      return query if query&.visible?(user)
    90	    end
    91	
    92	    # project default
    93	    query = project&.default_issue_query
    94	    return query if query&.visibility == VISIBILITY_PUBLIC
    95	
    96	    # global default
    97	    if (query_id = Setting.default_issue_query).present?
    98	      query = find_by(id: query_id)
    99	      return query if query&.visibility == VISIBILITY_PUBLIC
   100	    end
   101	    nil
   102	  end

   302	  def available_columns
   303	    return @available_columns if @available_columns
   304	
   305	    @available_columns = self.class.available_columns.dup
   306	    @available_columns += issue_custom_fields.visible.collect {|cf| QueryCustomFieldColumn.new(cf)}
   307	
   308	    if User.current.allowed_to?(:view_time_entries, project, :global => true)
   309	      # insert the columns after total_estimated_hours or at the end
   310	      index = @available_columns.find_index {|column| column.name == :total_estimated_hours}
   311	      index = (index ? index + 1 : -1)
   312	
   313	      subselect = "SELECT SUM(hours) FROM #{TimeEntry.table_name}" +
   314	        " JOIN #{Project.table_name} ON #{Project.table_name}.id = #{TimeEntry.table_name}.project_id" +
   315	        " WHERE (#{TimeEntry.visible_condition(User.current)}) AND #{TimeEntry.table_name}.issue_id = #{Issue.table_name}.id"
   316	
   317	      @available_columns.insert(
   318	        index,
   319	        QueryColumn.new(:spent_hours,
   320	                        :sortable => "COALESCE((#{subselect}), 0)",
   321	                        :default_order => 'desc',
   322	                        :caption => :label_spent_time,
   323	                        :totalable => true)
   324	      )
   325	
   326	      subselect = "SELECT SUM(hours) FROM #{TimeEntry.table_name}" +
   327	        " JOIN #{Project.table_name} ON #{Project.table_name}.id = #{TimeEntry.table_name}.project_id" +
   328	        " JOIN #{Issue.table_name} subtasks ON subtasks.id = #{TimeEntry.table_name}.issue_id" +
   329	        " WHERE (#{TimeEntry.visible_condition(User.current)})" +
   330	        " AND subtasks.root_id = #{Issue.table_name}.root_id AND subtasks.lft >= #{Issue.table_name}.lft AND subtasks.rgt <= #{Issue.table_name}.rgt"
   331	
   332	      @available_columns.insert(
   333	        index + 1,
   334	        QueryColumn.new(:total_spent_hours,
   335	                        :sortable => "COALESCE((#{subselect}), 0)",
   336	                        :default_order => 'desc',
   337	                        :caption => :label_total_spent_time)
   338	      )
   339	    end
   340	
   341	    if User.current.allowed_to?(:set_issues_private, nil, :global => true) ||
   342	      User.current.allowed_to?(:set_own_issues_private, nil, :global => true)
   343	      @available_columns <<
   344	        QueryColumn.new(:is_private,
   345	                        :sortable => "#{Issue.table_name}.is_private", :groupable => true)
   346	    end
   347	
   348	    disabled_fields = Tracker.disabled_core_fields(trackers).map {|field| field.delete_suffix('_id')}
   349	    if disabled_fields.include?("estimated_hours")
   350	      disabled_fields += %w[total_estimated_hours estimated_remaining_hours]
   351	    end
   352	    @available_columns.reject! do |column|
   353	      disabled_fields.include?(column.name.to_s)
   354	    end
   355	
   356	    @available_columns
   357	  end

   404	  # Returns the issues
   405	  # Valid options are :order, :offset, :limit, :include, :conditions
   406	  def issues(options={})
   407	    order_option = [group_by_sort_order, (options[:order] || sort_clause)].flatten.reject(&:blank?)
   408	    # The default order of IssueQuery is issues.id DESC(by IssueQuery#default_sort_criteria)
   409	    unless ["#{Issue.table_name}.id ASC", "#{Issue.table_name}.id DESC"].any?{|i| order_option.include?(i)}
   410	      order_option << "#{Issue.table_name}.id DESC"
   411	    end
   412	
   413	    scope = base_scope.
   414	      preload(:priority).
   415	      includes(([:status, :project] + (options[:include] || [])).uniq).
   416	      where(options[:conditions]).
   417	      order(order_option).
   418	      joins(joins_for_order_statement(order_option.join(','))).
   419	      limit(options[:limit]).
   420	      offset(options[:offset])
   421	
   422	    scope =
   423	      scope.preload(
   424	        [:tracker, :author, :assigned_to, :fixed_version,
   425	         :category, :attachments] & columns.map(&:name)
   426	      )
   427	    if has_custom_field_column?
   428	      scope = scope.preload(:custom_values)
   429	    end
   430	    if has_column?(:watcher_users)
   431	      scope = scope.preload(:watcher_users)
   432	    end
   433	
   434	    issues = scope.to_a
   435	
   436	    if has_column?(:spent_hours)
   437	      Issue.load_visible_spent_hours(issues)
   438	    end
   439	    if has_column?(:total_spent_hours)
   440	      Issue.load_visible_total_spent_hours(issues)
   441	    end
   442	    if has_column?(:last_updated_by)
   443	      Issue.load_visible_last_updated_by(issues)
   444	    end
   445	    if has_column?(:relations)
   446	      Issue.load_visible_relations(issues)
   447	    end
   448	    if has_column?(:last_notes)
   449	      Issue.load_visible_last_notes(issues)
   450	    end
   451	    issues
   452	  rescue ::ActiveRecord::StatementInvalid => e
   453	    raise StatementInvalid.new(e.message)
   454	  end

   505	  def sql_for_notes_field(field, operator, value)
   506	    subquery = "SELECT 1 FROM #{Journal.table_name}" +
   507	      " WHERE #{Journal.table_name}.journalized_type='Issue' AND #{Journal.table_name}.journalized_id=#{Issue.table_name}.id" +
   508	      " AND (#{sql_for_field field, operator.delete_prefix('!'), value, Journal.table_name, 'notes'})" +
   509	      " AND (#{Journal.visible_notes_condition(User.current, :skip_pre_condition => true)})"
   510	    "#{operator.start_with?('!') ? "NOT EXISTS" : "EXISTS"} (#{subquery})"
   511	  end
   512	
   513	  def sql_for_updated_by_field(field, operator, value)
   514	    neg = (operator == '!' ? 'NOT' : '')
   515	    subquery = "SELECT 1 FROM #{Journal.table_name}" +
   516	      " WHERE #{Journal.table_name}.journalized_type='Issue' AND #{Journal.table_name}.journalized_id=#{Issue.table_name}.id" +
   517	      " AND (#{sql_for_field field, '=', value, Journal.table_name, 'user_id'})" +
   518	      " AND (#{Journal.visible_notes_condition(User.current, :skip_pre_condition => true)})"
   519	
   520	    "#{neg} EXISTS (#{subquery})"
   521	  end
   522	
   523	  def sql_for_last_updated_by_field(field, operator, value)
   524	    neg = (operator == '!' ? 'NOT' : '')
   525	    subquery = "SELECT 1 FROM #{Journal.table_name} sj" +
   526	      " WHERE sj.journalized_type='Issue' AND sj.journalized_id=#{Issue.table_name}.id AND (#{sql_for_field field, '=', value, 'sj', 'user_id'})" +
   527	      " AND sj.id IN (SELECT MAX(#{Journal.table_name}.id) FROM #{Journal.table_name}" +
   528	      "   WHERE #{Journal.table_name}.journalized_type='Issue' AND #{Journal.table_name}.journalized_id=#{Issue.table_name}.id" +
   529	      "   AND (#{Journal.visible_notes_condition(User.current, :skip_pre_condition => true)}))"
   530	
   531	    "#{neg} EXISTS (#{subquery})"
   532	  end

   534	  def sql_for_estimated_hours_field(field, operator, value)
   535	    sql_for_field("estimated_hours", operator, value.map(&:to_hours), Issue.table_name, "estimated_hours")
   536	  end

   538	  def sql_for_spent_time_field(field, operator, value)
   539	    first, second = value.first.to_s.to_hours, value.second.to_s.to_hours
   540	    sql_op =
   541	      case operator
   542	      when "=", ">=", "<=" then  "#{operator} #{first}"
   543	      when "><"            then  "BETWEEN #{first} AND #{second}"
   544	      when "*"             then  "> 0"
   545	      when "!*"            then  "= 0"
   546	      else
   547	        return nil
   548	      end
   549	    "COALESCE((" +
   550	      "SELECT ROUND(CAST(SUM(hours) AS DECIMAL(30,3)), 2) " +
   551	      "FROM #{TimeEntry.table_name} " +
   552	      "WHERE issue_id = #{Issue.table_name}.id), 0) #{sql_op}"
   553	  end

   555	  def sql_for_watcher_id_field(field, operator, value)
   556	    db_table = Watcher.table_name
   557	    me_ids = [0, User.current.id]
   558	    me_ids.concat(User.current.groups.pluck(:id))
   559	    me, others = value.partition {|id| me_ids.include?(id.to_i)}
   560	    sql =
   561	      if others.any?
   562	        "SELECT #{Issue.table_name}.id FROM #{Issue.table_name} " +
   563	        "INNER JOIN #{db_table} ON #{Issue.table_name}.id = #{db_table}.watchable_id AND #{db_table}.watchable_type = 'Issue' " +
   564	        "LEFT OUTER JOIN #{Project.table_name} ON #{Project.table_name}.id = #{Issue.table_name}.project_id " +
   565	        "WHERE (" +
   566	          sql_for_field(field, '=', me, db_table, 'user_id') +
   567	        ') OR (' +
   568	          Project.allowed_to_condition(User.current, :view_issue_watchers) +
   569	          ' AND ' +
   570	          sql_for_field(field, '=', others, db_table, 'user_id') +
   571	        ')'
   572	      else
   573	        "SELECT #{db_table}.watchable_id FROM #{db_table} " +
   574	        "WHERE #{db_table}.watchable_type='Issue' AND " +
   575	        sql_for_field(field, '=', me, db_table, 'user_id')
   576	      end
   577	    "#{Issue.table_name}.id #{ operator == '=' ? 'IN' : 'NOT IN' } (#{sql})"
   578	  end

   580	  def sql_for_member_of_group_field(field, operator, value)
   581	    if operator == '*' # Any group
   582	      groups = Group.givable
   583	      operator = '=' # Override the operator since we want to find by assigned_to
   584	    elsif operator == "!*"
   585	      groups = Group.givable
   586	      operator = '!' # Override the operator since we want to find by assigned_to
   587	    else
   588	      groups = Group.where(:id => value).to_a
   589	    end
   590	    groups ||= []
   591	
   592	    members_of_groups = groups.inject([]) do |user_ids, group|
   593	      user_ids + group.user_ids + [group.id]
   594	    end.uniq.compact.sort.collect(&:to_s)
   595	
   596	    '(' + sql_for_field("assigned_to_id", operator, members_of_groups, Issue.table_name, "assigned_to_id", false) + ')'
   597	  end

   619	      subquery =

   189	  # Creates a new issue
   190	  def receive_issue
   191	    project = target_project
   192	
   193	    # Never receive emails to projects where adding issues is not possible
   194	    raise NotAllowedInProject, "not possible to add issues to project [#{project.name}]" unless project.allows_to?(:add_issues)
   195	
   196	    # check permission
   197	    unless handler_options[:no_permission_check]
   198	      raise InsufficientPermissions, "not allowed to add issues to project [#{project.name}]" unless user.allowed_to?(:add_issues, project)
   199	    end
   200	
   201	    issue = Issue.new(:author => user, :project => project)
   202	    attributes = issue_attributes_from_keywords(issue)
   203	    if handler_options[:no_permission_check]
   204	      issue.tracker_id = attributes['tracker_id']
   205	      if project
   206	        issue.tracker_id ||= project.trackers.first.try(:id)
   207	      end
   208	    end
   209	    issue.safe_attributes = attributes
   210	    issue.safe_attributes = {'custom_field_values' => custom_field_values_from_keywords(issue)}
   211	    issue.subject = cleaned_up_subject
   212	    if issue.subject.blank?
   213	      issue.subject = "(#{ll(Setting.default_language, :text_no_subject)})"
   214	    end
   215	    issue.description = cleaned_up_text_body
   216	    issue.start_date ||= User.current.today if Setting.default_issue_start_date_to_creation_date?
   217	    offset = Setting.default_issue_due_date_offset_in_days
   218	    issue.due_date ||= User.current.today + offset if offset
   219	    if handler_options[:issue][:is_private] == '1'
   220	      issue.is_private = true
   221	    end
   222	
   223	    # add To and Cc as watchers before saving so the watchers can reply to Redmine
   224	    add_watchers(issue)
   225	    issue.save!
   226	    add_attachments(issue)
   227	    logger&.info "MailHandler: issue ##{issue.id} created by #{user}"
   228	    issue
   229	  end

   231	  # Adds a note to an existing issue
   232	  def receive_issue_reply(issue_id, from_journal=nil)
   233	    issue = Issue.find_by(:id => issue_id)
   234	    if issue.nil?
   235	      raise MissingContainer, "reply to nonexistant issue [##{issue_id}]"
   236	    end
   237	
   238	    # Never receive emails to projects where adding issue notes is not possible
   239	    project = issue.project
   240	    raise NotAllowedInProject, "not possible to add notes to project [#{project.name}]" unless project.allows_to?(:add_issue_notes)
   241	
   242	    # check permission
   243	    unless handler_options[:no_permission_check]
   244	      unless issue.visible?(user) && issue.notes_addable?
   245	        raise InsufficientPermissions, "not allowed to add notes on issues to project [#{issue.project.name}]"
   246	      end
   247	    end
   248	
   249	    # ignore CLI-supplied defaults for new issues
   250	    handler_options[:issue] = {}
   251	
   252	    journal = issue.init_journal(user)
   253	    if from_journal && from_journal.private_notes?
   254	      # If the received email was a reply to a private note, make the added note private
   255	      issue.private_notes = true
   256	    end
   257	    issue.safe_attributes = issue_attributes_from_keywords(issue)
   258	    issue.safe_attributes = {'custom_field_values' => custom_field_values_from_keywords(issue)}
   259	    journal.notes = cleaned_up_text_body
   260	
   261	    # add To and Cc as watchers before saving so the watchers can reply to Redmine
   262	    add_watchers(issue)
   263	    issue.save!
   264	    add_attachments(issue)
   265	    logger&.info "MailHandler: issue ##{issue.id} updated by #{user}"
   266	    journal
   267	  end
   268	
   269	  # Reply will be added to the issue
   270	  def receive_journal_reply(journal_id)
   271	    journal = Journal.find_by(:id => journal_id)
   272	
   273	    if journal && journal.journalized_type == 'Issue'
   274	      receive_issue_reply(journal.journalized_id, journal)
   275	    elsif m = email.subject.to_s.match(ISSUE_REPLY_SUBJECT_RE)
   276	      logger&.info "MailHandler: reply to a nonexistant journal, calling receive_issue_reply with issue from subject"
   277	      receive_issue_reply(m[1].to_i)
   278	    else
   279	      raise MissingContainer, "reply to nonexistant journal [#{journal_id}]"
   280	    end
   281	  end

   283	  # Receives a reply to a forum message
   284	  def receive_message_reply(message_id)
   285	    message = Message.find_by(:id => message_id)&.root
   286	    if message.nil?
   287	      raise MissingContainer, "reply to nonexistant message [#{message_id}]"
   288	    end
   289	
   290	    # Never receive emails to projects where adding messages is not possible
   291	    project = message.project
   292	    raise NotAllowedInProject, "not possible to add messages to project [#{project.name}]" unless project.allows_to?(:add_messages)
   293	
   294	    unless handler_options[:no_permission_check]
   295	      raise InsufficientPermissions, "not allowed to add messages to project [#{message.project.name}]" unless user.allowed_to?(:add_messages, message.project)
   296	    end
   297	
   298	    if !message.locked?
   299	      reply = Message.new(:subject => cleaned_up_subject.gsub(%r{^.*msg\d+\]}, '').strip,
   300	                          :content => cleaned_up_text_body)
   301	      reply.author = user
   302	      reply.board = message.board
   303	      message.children << reply
   304	      add_attachments(reply)
   305	      reply
   306	    else
   307	      raise LockedTopic, "ignoring reply to a locked message [#{message.id} #{message.subject}]"
   308	    end
   309	  end
   310	
   311	  # Receives a reply to a news entry
   312	  def receive_news_reply(news_id)
   313	    news = News.find_by_id(news_id)
   314	    if news.nil?
   315	      raise MissingContainer, "reply to nonexistant news [#{news_id}]"
   316	    end
   317	
   318	    # Never receive emails to projects where adding news comments is not possible
   319	    project = news.project
   320	    raise NotAllowedInProject, "not possible to add news comments to project [#{project.name}]" unless project.allows_to?(:comment_news)
   321	
   322	    unless handler_options[:no_permission_check]
   323	      unless news.commentable?(user)
   324	        raise InsufficientPermissions, "not allowed to comment on news item [#{news.id} #{news.title}]"
   325	      end
   326	    end
   327	
   328	    comment = news.comments.new
   329	    comment.author = user
   330	    comment.comments = cleaned_up_text_body
   331	    comment.save!
   332	    comment
   333	  end

   335	  # Receives a reply to a comment to a news entry
   336	  def receive_comment_reply(comment_id)
   337	    comment = Comment.find_by_id(comment_id)
   338	
   339	    if comment && comment.commented_type == 'News'
   340	      receive_news_reply(comment.commented.id)
   341	    else
   342	      raise MissingContainer, "reply to nonexistant comment [#{comment_id}]"
   343	    end
   344	  end

   346	  def add_attachments(obj)
   347	    if email.attachments && email.attachments.any?
   348	      email.attachments.each do |attachment|
   349	        next unless accept_attachment?(attachment)
   350	        next unless attachment.body.decoded.size > 0
   351	
   352	        obj.attachments << Attachment.create(:container => obj,
   353	                          :file => attachment.body.decoded,
   354	                          :filename => attachment.filename,
   355	                          :author => user,
   356	                          :content_type => attachment.mime_type)
   357	      end
   358	    end
   359	  end
   360	
   360	
   361	  # Returns false if the +attachment+ of the incoming email should be ignored
   362	  def accept_attachment?(attachment)
   363	    @excluded ||= Setting.mail_handler_excluded_filenames.to_s.split(',').map(&:strip).reject(&:blank?)
   364	    @excluded.each do |pattern|
   365	      if Setting.mail_handler_enable_regex_excluded_filenames?
   366	        regexp = %r{\A#{pattern}\z}i
   367	      else
   368	        regexp = %r{\A#{Regexp.escape(pattern).gsub("\\*", ".*")}\z}i
   369	      end
   370	      if regexp.match?(attachment.filename.to_s)
   371	        logger.info "MailHandler: ignoring attachment #{attachment.filename} matching #{pattern}"
   372	        return false
   373	      end
   374	    end
   375	    true
   376	  end

   378	  # Adds To and Cc as watchers of the given object if the sender has the
   379	  # appropriate permission
   380	  def add_watchers(obj)
   381	    if handler_options[:no_permission_check] || user.allowed_to?(:"add_#{obj.class.name.underscore}_watchers", obj.project)
   382	      addresses = [email.to, email.cc].flatten.compact.uniq.collect {|a| a.strip.downcase}
   383	      unless addresses.empty?
   384	        users = User.active.having_mail(addresses).to_a
   385	        users -= obj.watcher_users
   386	        users.each do |u|
   387	          obj.add_watcher(u)
   388	        end
   389	      end
   390	    end
   391	  end

   393	  def get_keyword(attr, options={})
   394	    @keywords ||= {}
   395	    if @keywords.has_key?(attr)
   396	      @keywords[attr]
   397	    else
   398	      @keywords[attr] = begin
   399	        override =
   400	          if options.key?(:override)
   401	            options[:override]
   402	          else
   403	            handler_options[:allow_override].intersect?([attr.to_s.downcase.gsub(/\s+/, '_'), 'all'])
   404	          end
   405	        if override && (v = extract_keyword!(cleaned_up_text_body, attr, options[:format]))
   406	          v
   407	        elsif handler_options[:issue][attr].present?
   408	          handler_options[:issue][attr]
   409	        end
   410	      end
   411	    end
   412	  end
   413	
   414	  # Destructively extracts the value for +attr+ in +text+
   415	  # Returns nil if no matching keyword found
   416	  def extract_keyword!(text, attr, format=nil)
   417	    keys = [attr.to_s.humanize]
   418	    if attr.is_a?(Symbol)
   419	      if user && user.language.present?
   420	        keys << l("field_#{attr}", :default => '', :locale =>  user.language)
   421	      end
   422	      if Setting.default_language.present?
   423	        keys << l("field_#{attr}", :default => '', :locale =>  Setting.default_language)
   424	      end
   425	    end
   426	    keys.reject! {|k| k.blank?}
   427	    keys.collect! {|k| Regexp.escape(k)}
   428	    format ||= '.+'
   429	    keyword = nil
   430	    regexp = /^(#{keys.join('|')})[ \t]*:[ \t]*(#{format})\s*$/i
   431	    if m = text.match(regexp)
   432	      keyword = m[2].strip
   433	      text.sub!(regexp, '')
   434	    end
   435	    keyword
   436	  end

   438	  def get_project_from_receiver_addresses
   439	    local, domain = handler_options[:project_from_subaddress].to_s.split("@")
   440	    return nil unless local && domain
   441	
   442	    local = Regexp.escape(local)
   443	
   444	    [:to, :cc, :bcc].each do |field|
   445	      header = @email[field]
   446	      next if header.blank? || header.field.blank? || !header.field.respond_to?(:addrs)
   447	
   448	      header.field.addrs.each do |addr|
   449	        if addr.domain.to_s.casecmp(domain)==0 && addr.local.to_s =~ /\A#{local}\+([^+]+)\z/
   450	          if project = Project.find_by_identifier($1)
   451	            return project
   452	          end
   453	        end
   454	      end
   455	    end
   456	    nil
   457	  end
   458	
   459	  def target_project
   460	    # TODO: other ways to specify project:
   461	    # * parse the email To field
   462	    # * specific project (eg. Setting.mail_handler_target_project)
   463	    target = get_project_from_receiver_addresses
   464	    target ||= Project.find_by_identifier(get_keyword(:project))
   465	    if target.nil?
   466	      # Invalid project keyword, use the project specified as the default one
   467	      default_project = handler_options[:issue][:project]
   468	      if default_project.present?
   469	        target = Project.find_by_identifier(default_project)
   470	      end

   107	
   108	  # Triggers the given event for the given object, scheduling qualifying hooks
   109	  # to be called.
   110	  def self.trigger(event, object)
   111	    return unless enabled?
   112	
   113	    hooks_for(event, object).each do |hook|
   114	      payload = hook.payload(event, object)
   115	      WebhookJob.perform_later(hook.id, payload.to_json)
   116	    end
   117	  end
   118	
   119	  # Finds hooks for the given event and object.
   120	  # Returns an array of hooks that are active, have the given event in their list
   121	  # of events, and whose user can see the object.
   122	  #
   123	  # Object must have a project_id and respond to visible?(user)
   124	  def self.hooks_for(event, object)
   125	    Webhook.active
   126	      .joins("INNER JOIN projects_webhooks on projects_webhooks.webhook_id = webhooks.id")
   127	      .eager_load(:user)
   128	      .where(users: { status: User::STATUS_ACTIVE }, projects_webhooks: { project_id: object.project_id })
   129	      .to_a.select do |hook|
   130	      hook.events.include?(event) && object.visible?(hook.user) && hook.user.allowed_to?(:use_webhooks, object.project)
   131	    end
   132	  end
   133

    23	  def webhook_payload(user, action)
    24	    h = super
    25	    if action == 'updated' && current_journal.present?
    26	      journal = journals.visible(user).find_by_id(current_journal.id)
    27	      if journal.present?
    28	        h[:data][:journal] = journal_payload(journal, user)
    29	        h[:timestamp] = journal.created_on.iso8601
    30	      end
    31	    end
    32	    h
    33	  end

    37	  def journal_payload(journal, user)
    38	    {
    39	      id: journal.id,
    40	      created_on: journal.created_on.iso8601,
    41	      notes: journal.notes,
    42	      user: {
    43	        id: journal.user.id,
    44	        name: journal.user.name,
    45	      },
    46	      details: journal.visible_details(user).map do |d|
    47	        {
    48	          property: d.property,
    49	          prop_key: d.prop_key,
    50	          old_value: d.old_value,
    51	          value: d.value,
    52	        }
    53	      end
    54	    }
    55	  end
